Japanese: ９月　８日　午前１１時４３分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: September 8, 11:43 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: き‥‥記憶喪失！
English: A-Amnesia!?

Japanese: そ、そんな状態で アタシの弁護を‥‥。
English: I can't believe my lawyer's trying to defend me in such a state...

Japanese: ‥‥まあね。
どーして 言ってくれなかったッスかッ！
English: Why didn't you tell me, sir!?

Japanese: ‥‥ごめん。 なんか、言い出せなくて‥‥。
English: I'm sorry I didn't mention it to you.

Japanese: ‥‥あッ、そうだ！
English: Oh! I know what to do!

Japanese: スゴいショックを与えると なおるコトがあるらしいッス！
English: I heard you can fix something like this with a really strong shock to your system!

Japanese: さ。さ。アタマ出してください！ マコ・キックを食らわすッス！
English: Come on, lower your head a little! A Maggey Kick should be all you need!

Japanese: い、いやいやいや！ エンリョしておくよ。
English: Ah, no, no, no. I think I'll pass on this one.

Japanese: あ‥‥スミマセン。
English: Come on!... Ah, I'm sorry.

Japanese: スズキ、困ってるヒトを見ると ほっとけないッス‥‥。
English: Whenever I see someone in trouble, I have a hard time leaving them alone...

Japanese: つい、おせっかい焼いて、 すべてをブチ壊すッス。
English: I tend to stick my nose where it doesn't belong and try to tackle everyone's problems.

Japanese: （‥‥このトシでアタマを ブチ壊されちゃたまらないな）
English: (Well, my head's one problem you won't be tackling today...)

Japanese: とにかく今は、きみのピンチを 切り抜けるほうが先だよ。
English: Well, we're here to solve your problem first. We can deal with mine later.

Japanese: ぼくが知らないことは、 なんでも教えてくれないか？
English: For now, do you think you can fill me in on a few things?

Japanese: カンゲキです！
English: Of course! I'd be honored to!

Japanese: じゃあ、ええと‥‥。 まず、自己紹介からいきますねッ！
English: Ah, well, I guess we'll start with my name and then I can tell you about me!

Japanese: ‥‥いやいやいや。 さすがにそれは、わかってるよ。
English: No, no, that's ok. Really. I think I know you and your name pretty well by now.

Japanese: ‥‥どっちかと言うと、 ぼく自身のほうがナゾなんだけど。
English: I was wondering if you could help me figure out a few things about myself.

Japanese: “ナルホドー”だっけ？ ‥‥ヘンな名前。
English: So, my name is "Phoenix Wright"? What a weird name.

Japanese: ううう。重症ッス‥‥。
English: Hmmmm... This is serious. You really don't remember.

Japanese: しかたありませんね‥‥。 これ、お貸しするッス。
English: I'll tell you what, sir. You can have this back, and maybe it'll help!
This is... a business card?

Japanese: 成歩堂さんからもらった、 スズキの宝物ッス。
English: I got this from you. It's my most prized possession!

Japanese: あとで返してくださいねッ！
English: You can borrow it for now, but please give it back, OK!?

Japanese: う、うん。 （ウラに数字が書いてある‥‥）
English: OK. (There are some numbers written on the back...)

Japanese: あ、それは携帯電話の番号ッス。 ‥‥成歩堂さんの。
English: Oh, that's your cell phone number!

Japanese: 証拠品《成歩堂の名刺》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Phoenix's Business Card added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ま。これでいちおう、 ナルホド気分にはなれるから、
English: I guess for now, we should stop talking about me,

Japanese: 今度は事件のことだ。
English: and start talking about this case.

Japanese: 事件のこと‥‥ですか？
English: This case...?

Japanese: そう。‥‥手がかりに なりそうな話、ない？
English: Yup. Can you think of anything that would be helpful for me to know?

Japanese: 手がかり‥‥ うーん‥‥そうですねー‥‥
English: Um, what can I tell you...? Ah, um... Hmm...

Japanese: あの携帯電話のことぐらいしか、 思い当たりませんケド‥‥
English: I can't think of anything other than the incident with that cell phone, but...

Japanese: ‥‥携帯電話？
English: ...Cell phone?

Japanese: はッ。面会のときお話ししたら、 成歩堂さん、目の色変えてたッス！
English: Yeah! Your eyes lit up when we talked about it at the Detention Center, sir!

Japanese: 聞かせてくれ！　大急ぎで。 ホラ早く！
English: Hurry up then and tell me! This might be very important!

Japanese: はッ！　了解ッス！
English: OK! Roger!

Japanese: ‥‥事件のあった日、 午後６時前のことでした。
English: It was on the day of the crime, just before 6 PM...

Japanese: 町尾さんと歩いていたら、 携帯電話を拾ったんです。
English: I picked up a lost cell phone while on a walk with Dustin.

Japanese: ‥‥急にその電話が 鳴り出しちゃって‥‥。
English: All of a sudden, the phone began to ring...

Japanese: 『あ、あの、もしもし。 スズキと申しますが‥‥』
English: "Um, hello?"

Japanese: 『あ。すみません。その携帯電話を 落とした者なんですけど‥‥』
English: "Oh, thank you! I've been searching for my phone."

Japanese: 『よかった！　じゃァさっそく お会いして、お返ししますッ！』
English: "Is this yours? Oh, I'm glad you called! We can meet up and I can give this back!"

Japanese: 『すぐ、そちらにうかがいます。 もう一度、お名前を‥‥』
English: "I'll be right there, um... I'm sorry, I didn't catch your name..."

Japanese: 『はッ！　スズキ　マコッス！』
English: "You can call me Maggey!"

Japanese: ‥‥で、６時に会う ヤクソクをしたんです。
English: We agreed to meet up at 6 PM.

Japanese: だから、町尾さんといっしょに 待ってたんですけど‥‥
English: Dustin and I waited for the person to show up...

Japanese: ケッキョクその人、 来なかったんです。
English: but they never did.

Japanese: ふーん‥‥。
English: Hmmmm...

Japanese: それで、拾った携帯電話は？ 今、どこに‥‥。
English: So where is the phone you found now?

Japanese: きのう、成歩堂さんに おわたししました！
English: I gave it to you yesterday!

Japanese: え‥‥？　ぼくに？ （ポケット、かな‥‥）
English: Huh? To me? (Is it that phone in my pocket...?)

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥これか。
English: Y-You mean this?

Japanese: 事件に関係あるんですか？　それ。
English: Do you think it has anything to do with the murder?

Japanese: さあ。‥‥でも、きのうの ぼくが目の色を変えたんなら‥‥
English: I... don't really know... But if my eyes "lit up"...

Japanese: あー。こんなところにいたよー！
English: AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHHHH!! YOU WERE HERE ALL ALONG!!

Japanese: もう、ひどいなあ！
English: You're so mean!!

Japanese: 携帯電話にかけても 出てくれないし！
English: I called you a million times but you wouldn't pick up!

Japanese: 法廷に行ってみたら、 だれもいないし‥‥
English: And when I went to check in the courtroom, everyone had already left...

Japanese: （うわ‥‥。 またヘンなのが出てきたぞ‥‥）
English: (Ack! Now who in the heck is this?)

Japanese: （まさか、この子もぼくの 知り合いなのか‥‥？）
English: (Let me guess. I'm supposed to know this girl too...)

Japanese: あ、マコちゃん！ おはよう！
English: Hey, good morning, Maggey!

Japanese: マヨイさん、 おはようございますッ！
English: And a good morning to you, too, Maya!

Japanese: どう？　どう？ 調子のほうは！
English: So!? So!? How's it going!?

Japanese: 下の下、といったところッス。
English: Is there a word for "worse than abysmal"...?

Japanese: ふーん‥‥。 でも、もうだいじょうぶ！
English: Oh? And what if I said that everything will be fine?

Japanese: この真宵ちゃんが、シッカリ証拠 持ってきてあげたからね！
English: That's right! It's Maya to the rescue with the ultra-decisive super-important evidence!

Japanese: はい、なるほどくん！ お待ちかねのモノ！
English: Here you are, Nick! The thing you wanted me to bring!

Japanese: え？　あ？　ああ。ありがと‥‥。 （なんだこりゃ‥‥リスト？）
English: Huh? Oh, ah, thanks... (What the heck is this? A list?)

Japanese: （ヒトの名前と電話番号が、 ２０人ぶんぐらい並んでいる）
English: (It has about 20 people's names and phone numbers written on it.)

Japanese: ホント、調べるの タイヘンだったんだからね！
English: It was kind of tough, but I managed to find out some dirt!

Japanese: なんか、そのヒトたち、 かなりのワルみたいだよ。
English: It looks like these guys are up to no good.
"No good"? As in?

Japanese: 警察が今、捜査している、 詐欺グループがあってさ。
English: There's a group of con artists the police are currently investigating.

Japanese: そのメンバーじゃないか、 って思われてる人たちみたい。
English: I think these guys are members of that group.

Japanese: 証拠品《リスト》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Names List added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥なんでこんなところに 詐欺グループが出てくるんだろ？
English: Why would a group of con artists pop up in a case like this?

Japanese: あ、あたしに聞かないでよ！
English: Don't look at me!

Japanese: ううん‥‥。だいいち、 このリストは、どこで‥‥？
English: Hmm... And where did you get this list from in the first place?

Japanese: うええええッ！ ナニ言ってんの！
English: Whaaaaat!? Why are you asking that!?

Japanese: きのう、それをわたしてくれたの、 なるほどくんじゃない！
English: You're the one who asked me to look this up yesterday!

Japanese: あ‥‥そうなの？
English: Oh... is that right?

Japanese: マコちゃんが拾った携帯電話に 登録されていた電話番号だよ。
English: These numbers were in the memory of that phone Maggey found.

Japanese: ふうん‥‥そうだったんだ‥‥。
English: Hmm, so that's where they're from.

Japanese: なるほどくんさあ、 最近忘れっぽいよねー。
English: You're awfully forgetful these days, Nick.

Japanese: やっぱり、トシはとりたくないよ！
English: I hope I never get to be a forgetful old prune like you!

Japanese: あの‥‥真宵さん。 じつは、成歩堂さんは‥‥
English: Um, Maya... Actually, Mr. Wright is...

Japanese: 弁護人！ そろそろ休憩時間が終わります。
English: Mr. Wright! Recess is now over.

Japanese: 被告人とともに、至急 法廷にもどってください！
English: Please bring the defendant and return to the courtroom immediately!

Japanese: あ、ほら。 話はあとあと。行こ！
English: Oh, oops! Guess you have to get going! We can talk about you being old later, Nick!
W-Wish us luck!

Japanese: （‥‥これで、どうやら材料は そろったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (I guess I have all the pieces now... More or less.)

Japanese: （あとは‥‥ぼくしだい。 負けられない‥‥ゼッタイに！）
English: (All that's left is to put it all together. I'm not going to lose this. I can't!)

Japanese: ほらほら、なるほどくん。 早くする！
English: Come on, Nick. Better get a move on!

Japanese: う、うん。
English: Y-Yeah.

Japanese: 同日　午前１１時５４分 地方裁判所　第２法廷
English: September 8, 11:54 AM District Court Courtroom No. 2

Japanese: では、審理を再開しましょう。 証人を入廷させてください。
English: The court will now reconvene. Please call your next witness to the stand, Mr. Payne.

Japanese: はっ。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 裁判長、その前にちょっと‥‥。
English: But before I do, if I may say a few words...

Japanese: なんでしょう？
English: What is it, Mr. Payne?

Japanese: そのう‥‥。 これから呼ぶ証人なんですけども。
English: It's about the next witness.

Japanese: 妙に人の神経をさかなでする ところがありまして、その。
English: He has a tendency to say things that rub people the wrong way, you see,

Japanese: どうか、あたたかい目で‥‥
English: so I ask that the court might be a little lenient on...

Japanese: ‥‥前置きはいりません。 早く呼んでください。
English: There is no need to give a preface. Just hurry up and call your witness, please.

Japanese: ははッ。‥‥それでは。
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor. ...The prosecution calls its next witness;

Japanese: 事件当日、現場の公園をさんぽして いた浪人生を入廷させてください！
English: a drifter who was taking a walk in the park on the day of the murder!

Japanese: 証人。まず、名前を‥‥。
English: Please state your name for the court, witness.

Japanese: その前に、ヒトコトいいですか？
English: Before I do, I'd like to clarify a little something.

Japanese: は？　‥‥ど、どうぞ。
English: Huh? Oh, alright, go ahead.

Japanese: 先ほどアナタは、このボクを こう紹介されましたね？
English: Just now, you introduced my wonderful self to the court, correct?

Japanese: “さんぽ中の浪人生”と！
English: Perhaps as a "drifter who was taking a walk"?

Japanese: ‥‥え？ そ、そうでしたっけ‥‥？
English: D-Did I?

Japanese: コマりますね。人をそういう 色メガネで判断されては。
English: But I will not stand for that! Now you've tinted the court's eyes and colored me wrongly.

Japanese: たしかにボクは、大学生と呼ばれる 身分ではないかもしれない。
English: Sure, I suppose calling me a university student would not be the absolute truth,

Japanese: 百歩ゆずって、 それは認めてあげましょう。
English: but to give in and just settle would be as evil as death and I can't have that!

Japanese: しかしボクは、すべてが一流で なければならない宿命なのです。
English: Everything in my life is to be of the utmost, highest, top grade quality, you understand.

Japanese: そんなボクにとってふさわしい 大学とは、どこなのか‥‥？
English: I am merely looking for that perfect, top notch, unbeatable university, don't you see...?

Japanese: 慎重に検討している最中なのです。 それをアナタは“さんぽ中”‥‥
English: I have a rigorous selection process and I was in serious thought during my "walk" as...

Japanese: わわ、わかりました。 申しわけない。気をつけます。
English: Yes, yes, I understand. I'm very sorry. I will be more careful from now on.

Japanese: なな、なんか スゴい人が出てきたね。
English: Wh-What is he? A human chatterbox?

Japanese: やれやれ。 アレに尋問するのかよ‥‥。
English: Ugh... I have to question HIM?

Japanese: 一流のファッション！　車！　女！ メガネ！　そしてモチロン、大学！
English: Fashion! Cars! Women! Glasses! And of course, University! First-rates only need apply!

Japanese: （メガネなんか かけてないじゃないか‥‥）
English: (Glasses...? But you aren't wearing glasses...)

Japanese: もうけっこうです！ 証人は、さっさと名前を‥‥
English: That's enough! Your name, witness.

Japanese: ははあ。そう来るワケですか。
English: Oh? Is that how you want to play this?

Japanese: 権力をふりかざして、若い芽を つみとろうという寸法だ。
English: Using your power and influence to keep the young people down. I see how you work now.

Japanese: 老人たち特有のキタナイやりかた。 イカガなものかと思いますがね。
English: You old people and your dirty tricks. You thought you had me, but you thought wrong.

Japanese: すす、すみません。 以後、気をつけます。
English: I-I'm sorry. It won't happen again.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Oh man...)

Japanese: ‥‥まあいいでしょう。 つきあってあげますよ。
English: I forgive you. Alright, I suppose I can tell you my name.

Japanese: ボクの名前は、諸平野　貴雅 （もろへいやたかまさ）です。
English: I am Richard Wellington, the "Drifting Virtuoso" with a Ph.D. in Drifting, as it were.

Japanese: “ほぼ大学生”と呼んでもらっても ベツにボクはかまいませんよ。
English: If you wanted to, you could call me a "University Student in Transit".

Japanese: モロヘイヤさん。
English: Ahem, Mr. Wellington.

Japanese: 事件当日、現場のわんぱく公園を さんぽ‥‥散策していましたね？
English: On the day of the murder, you were taking a... er, strolling through the park, correct?

Japanese: まあ、アナタがどうしても そう言いたいのならば、どうぞ。
English: It would appear that you are attached to that word. If you must, then by all means.

Japanese: それを認めないほど、ボクも コドモじゃありませんからね。
English: But I remind you that I am in no way a prepubescent boy, "out on a walk" with mommy.

Japanese: だいたいボクは‥‥
English: If you must know, I am--

Japanese: とにかく、あなたがさんぽ中に 見たことを証言してください！
English: Anyway! Please testify to this court what you saw during your walk through the park!

Japanese: あ、ホラ。また言った！ “さんぽ中”‥‥
English: See, you said it again! "Taking a walk"... You know, you--

Japanese: とにかく、目撃したことを 証言してくださいッ！
English: What you witnessed will do, Mr. Wellington!

Japanese: ～事件当日、目撃したこと～
English: -- What I Saw That Day --

Japanese: 午後はずっと、公園で 思索にふけっていましたね。
English: I was at the park all afternoon, deep in thought about my life situation.

Japanese: 時間はよくおぼえてないけど‥‥ 夕方の６時すぎのコトでしたか。
English: I don't remember the time all that well, but I do believe it was past 6 PM.

Japanese: いきなり目の前に 警官が転げ落ちてきたんですよ。
English: All of a sudden, a police officer falls from above, right in front of my eyes.

Japanese: 思わず見上げると、真っ青なカオを した女と目が合いましたね。
English: Without a thought, I looked up, and there I met the eyes of a charming, young lady.

Japanese: もちろんおぼえています。 被告席の彼女でしたねあれは。
English: Of course I remember her sweet face. It was that of the pretty defendant there.

Japanese: 他に現場で変わったことといえば、 バナナが落ちていましたね。
English: The only other thing I saw was the banana that fell with the police officer.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 これはかなり、決定的ですね‥‥。
English: Hmm, that was certainly a decisive testimony.

Japanese: けけ、決定的だって！ なるほどくん。
English: Decisive!? Nick, did you hear what he just said!?

Japanese: ‥‥ふうん。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: ふうん、って！ なんでそんなに落ちついてるの！
English: That's all you have to say? How can you be so calm!?

Japanese: （フシギだ‥‥。 ココロが妙におだやかだぞ‥‥）
English: (It's strange... My mind is very calm and clear.)

Japanese: たぶん‥‥信じてるんだよ。 依頼人のこと。
English: Maybe it's because I... believe in my client.

Japanese: マコちゃん‥‥？
English: You mean Maggey?

Japanese: そう。‥‥彼女が無実ならば、 答えは１つしかない。
English: Yes. And if she really is innocent, then that can only mean one thing:

Japanese: ‥‥アイツは、ウソをついている！
English: That guy is lying!

Japanese: では、弁護人。 ‥‥尋問をおねがいします。
English: You may now question the witness, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （かならず見破ってやるさ。 ‥‥どんなうまいウソでもな！）
English: (I'll find out the truth, no matter how well you craft your lies!)

Japanese: ～事件当日、目撃したこと～
English: -- What I Saw That Day --

Japanese: 午後はずっと、公園で 思索にふけっていましたね。
English: I was at the park all afternoon, deep in thought about my life situation.

Japanese: 時間はよくおぼえてないけど‥‥ 夕方の６時すぎのコトでしたか。
English: I don't remember the time all that well, but I do believe it was past 6 PM.

Japanese: いきなり目の前に 警官が転げ落ちてきたんですよ。
English: All of a sudden, a police officer falls from above, right in front of my eyes.

Japanese: 思わず見上げると、真っ青なカオを した女と目が合いましたね。
English: Without a thought, I looked up, and there I met the eyes of a charming, young lady.

Japanese: もちろんおぼえています。 被告席の彼女でしたねあれは。
English: Of course I remember her sweet face. It was that of the pretty defendant there.

Japanese: 他に現場で変わったことといえば、 バナナが落ちていましたね。
English: The only other thing I saw was the banana that fell with the police officer.

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくの依頼人が無実なら、 アイツが目撃できるハズはない）
English: (If my client is innocent, there is no way he could've seen what he says he did.)

Japanese: つまり、今の証言がウソだ、 って証明できればいいんだ！
English: Which means if we can somehow show he's lying...

Japanese: そういうことだね。
English: Yeah, that's exactly what we need do.

Japanese: （って、すっかりなじんじゃってる けど、だれなんだ、この子‥‥）
English: (She's right. She's got a sharp mind, but I just wish I could remember who she is...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうしたの？
English: Is everything OK, Nick...?

Japanese: ずっと公園に？ ‥‥ずいぶんヒマですね。
English: So you were at the park all afternoon? You seem to have a lot of free time.

Japanese: これはこれは シッケイな男だなキミは。
English: Hmph. That was very rude of you. But then again, what can I expect?

Japanese: どうせ、名もないゴミのような 大学を出たんだろうね。
English: That's what you get from a man who graduated from a no-name, trashy university.

Japanese: ゴ、ゴミ‥‥
English: N-No name? Trashy...?

Japanese: アタマがギザギザのサルくんには ムズカシイかもしれないが、
English: Now, this might be hard for a mush-headed, feeble-minded baboon like you, but

Japanese: この国の未来について 思案をめぐらせていたのさ。
English: I have to think very carefully about the future of our great country.

Japanese: さっきは、どの大学に入るか 考えていた、とか‥‥
English: But I thought you said you were thinking about which college to go to just now...

Japanese: ナーンセンス！
English: Oh, puh-leaze.

Japanese: ボクがどの大学に入るか‥‥？ それがこの国の未来を決めるのさ。
English: Which university I go to will directly affect the very future of this country!

Japanese: （ううう‥‥ムカつく）
English: (That arrogant little snot...)

Japanese: どうして時間がわかったんですか？ 腕時計はしていないみたいですが。
English: How did you know what time it was? I see you're not wearing a watch, so...

Japanese: ‥‥ふう。それでボクを ヤリこめたツモリかい？
English: Is that the best you can do? Do you think you can discredit me like that?

Japanese: 三流のヒガミというヤツだ。 イカガなものかと思うけどね。
English: You're just a third-rate, biased fool. I guess I can't expect real smarts from you.

Japanese: （ムカつく男だなあ‥‥）
English: (Grr... His arrogance is really intolerable... So, what should I do now...?)

Japanese: （まあ‥‥、これをゆさぶっても しかたないな）
English: (Well, I guess there's no point in pressing him further.)

Japanese: （だって現場には、時計台が あったんだから‥‥）
English: (After all, there was a clock right there at the crime scene...)

Japanese: どうして時間がわかったか、 聞いてるんです！
English: Answer the question! How did you know what time it was!?

Japanese: ‥‥ヤレヤレ。 ホントに困ったことだよ。
English: Tsk, tsk. I can't believe I have to deal with a worm like you.

Japanese: 中身がないヤツにかぎって 机を叩いたりゴミ箱をけったり。
English: You're just a shallow man who can only slam on desks and point at people for fun.

Japanese: ‥‥しかたないな。
English: Hmph, I guess I don't have a choice.

Japanese: 三流大学出のキミにも わかるレベルで説明してあげよう。
English: I'll try to explain it so that even a third-rate simpleton like you can understand.

Japanese: ‥‥わんぱく公園にはね。 時計台というものがあるんだよ。
English: There was this little thing they call a "clock" at the park.

Japanese: わかるかな‥‥時計台。 台がついた時計のことさ。
English: Did you get that? Do you know what a clock is? It's a thing that tells you the time.

Japanese: ‥‥現場の写真にも 写っていますね。
English: As you can see, Mr. Wright, it's even in this picture of the crime scene.

Japanese: （ううう‥‥たしかに‥‥）
English: (Oh... So it is... Urk.)

Japanese: ま。その時計を見たワケだけど。 ‥‥ボクに言わせれば、
English: I looked at that clock, and that's how I knew the time.

Japanese: 時計などというモノで 時間を切り刻むなんて、
English: But if you ask me, this whole concept of breaking time apart into fragments...

Japanese: ナンセンスきわまりないね。
English: It's total and utter nonsense that no man should follow.

Japanese: 一流のニンゲンは、 時計のために生きたりはしない。
English: A real first-class person doesn't live by, nor is he chained by, time.

Japanese: 腕時計？　ハッ！　愚劣だね！
English: And to wear a watch? Hah! What a ridiculous notion!

Japanese: もっと自由に生きるコトを おすすめするよ！
English: People should live freely without constraints. That's how life should be!

Japanese: （‥‥ヘタなツッコミを 入れたばっかりに‥‥）
English: (And yet again, another flood of meaningless words...)

Japanese: （壮大に時間をムダに しちまったな‥‥）
English: (Talk about a first-class waste of time...)

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、とにかく‥‥。
English: In any case...

Japanese: どうして警官だとわかりました？
English: And how did you know he was a police officer?

Japanese: オヤオヤ。このボクの推理力を 知らないのかい。
English: You obviously have no idea how powerful my deductive reasoning skills are.

Japanese: ひとめ見れば、職業ぐらいピタリ！ ‥‥当ててしまうよ。
English: With one glance, I could tell just what kind of occupation he held.

Japanese: あのヘアスタイルに、手のカタチ。 典型的な警察官タイプだった。
English: That shoddy, do-it-yourself hairstyle practically screamed "I'm a police officer".

Japanese: ネクタイのゆがみ具合もポイント。 それに安物のクツ。
English: It was also the way he tied his tie and those cheap, low- quality shoes. Ugh.

Japanese: ついでに、警官の制服を 着ていたからね。
English: Oh, and I suppose it was also because he was wearing an officer's uniform.

Japanese: （‥‥それを先に言えよ！）
English: (Shouldn't that statement have come first!?)

Japanese: へえ。すごいなあ。
English: Wow, that's pretty impressive.

Japanese: ね。ね。あたしのコトも わかっちゃうのかな？
English: Hey, Nick! Do you think he's figured out what I do?

Japanese: （‥‥その前に ぼくが聞きたいぞ‥‥）
English: (Even I haven't figured that out yet...)

Japanese: その女性のことが、ハッキリ 見えたんですか？
English: Are you sure you got a good look at her face?

Japanese: ニンゲンには、“目”というものが ついてるからねえ。
English: Animals have this thing called an "eye", Mr. Wright. They use this "eye" to see things.

Japanese: ‥‥それも、２つ。
English: In the case of humans, we have two of them. Yes, even you!

Japanese: だから、ハッキリ見えたかって 聞いてるんです！
English: I don't care if I have them or not! Did you or did you not get a clear look at her face!?

Japanese: ‥‥それはこれから 証言します。
English: That's what the witness was just about to get to.

Japanese: 大声を出さないで いただきたいね。
English: I would like to request that Mr. Wright not use such a loud voice during questioning.

Japanese: 大声を出さないように。
English: Sustained. Mr. Wright, please refrain from raising your voice in this court.

Japanese: （大声を出させないでくれえ）
English: (Then please don't make me have to raise my voice.)

Japanese: そろそろいいかな？ つづけても。
English: Are you finished? I'd like to continue, if that's alright with you.

Japanese: まちがいありませんか！
English: So you're SURE you are not mistaken?

Japanese: キミのまちがいだらけの人生と ボクを、いっしょにしないでくれ。
English: Please. Don't confuse your pitiful, train-wreck of a life with mine.

Japanese: ボクはね。いわゆるブランド品だ。 ニンゲンとして、ね。
English: I'm what you call a famous brand-name product, while you are only a cheap imitation.

Japanese: まちがいなんて、あり得ないよ。
English: There is no way someone as magnificent as myself could have made a mistake.

Japanese: あり得ないそうです。
English: Of course, of course.

Japanese: （とほほ‥‥）
English: (Oh ho ho ho. Of course.)

Japanese: 他に何か、気づいたことは ありませんでしたかな？
English: Did you notice anything else of interest, witness?

Japanese: ‥‥バナナ？
English: The banana...?

Japanese: しかも１本じゃない。 フサごとまるまるだったね。
English: Well, it was actually more than just one. More like a bunch of bananas.

Japanese: なんで、そんなものが‥‥？
English: Now what would a bunch of bananas be doing there...?

Japanese: ‥‥ふう、ヤレヤレ。 知るわけないだろ、ボクが。
English: And why would I know such a thing? I'm only telling you what I saw.

Japanese: ヘンだね。
English: That's really strange.

Japanese: バナナのコトなんて、 マコちゃんヒトコトも‥‥。
English: Maggey never mentioned anything about a bunch of bananas.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ きっと、ウソだよバナナなんて！
English: That's it, Nick! He's gotta be lying about the bananas!

Japanese: （‥‥ううん。 そうは言っても‥‥）
English: (Hmm... He could be, but...)

Japanese: （“現場でバナナを見た” なんてウソ、意味がないぞ‥‥）
English: (there's no reason for him to lie about there being bananas at the crime scene.)

Japanese: ‥‥もし、 ウソじゃなかったとしたら？
English: And what if it's not a lie?

Japanese: カンちがい‥‥かな。 何かと見まちがえた、とか。
English: Well, maybe he thought he was seeing one thing, and it was something else...?

Japanese: （‥‥カンちがい。それって やっぱり、ムジュンだよな‥‥）
English: (If he mistook something else for a bunch of bananas, then that would be an inaccuracy.)

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥）
English: (Think Phoenix, think!)

Japanese: ‥‥モロヘイヤさん。
English: Mr. Wellington.

Japanese: じつは、ここにあるんですよ。 ‥‥そのときのバナナが。
English: I believe I have the bananas you saw... right here!

Japanese: なんだ。キミもバナナのコト、 知ってたんじゃないか。
English: Ah, so you knew about the bananas, too. Why didn't you say so earlier?

Japanese: そんなことでボクを引っかけた なんて、思わないでほしいね。
English: But don't think you can use this as a way to pull more information out of me.

Japanese: （引っかかってるんだよな‥‥ 思いっきり！）
English: (And that's where you'd be wrong.)

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂くん‥‥ そ、それは‥‥
English: M-Mr. Wright. What is the meaning of this?

Japanese: グローブじゃないですか！
English: Isn't that the baseball glove?

Japanese: え‥‥！　な、なんだって？ グローブ‥‥？
English: Huh!? Wh-Wh-What!? A baseball glove??

Japanese: なんなら、食べてみますか？
English: Doesn't it look delicious? Care for a bite?

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな‥‥。
English: Th-That's...

Japanese: そんなバナ‥‥バカなああぁァッ！
English: That's not... It's a... Noooooooo!

Japanese: 裁判長！　これで、 １つハッキリしたことがあります！
English: Your Honor! I think this proves one very important fact!

Japanese: つまり、この証人は‥‥
English: This witness...

Japanese: モロヘイヤさんは、 大のバナナ好きなのです！
English: Mr. Wellington loves large bananas!

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Uh, Nick...

Japanese: こおりついちゃったじゃない！ ‥‥空気が！
English: I hope you've noticed the icy glares we're getting from everyone in here!

Japanese: そ‥‥それがいったい、 どうだと言うんですか？
English: Wh-What in the world do you mean?

Japanese: ちなみに、ボクはバナナより イチゴの方が好きだけど。
English: I'll have you know I like strawberries much better than bananas.

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Whoops...

Japanese: もう一度。 考えなおしてください。
English: Think it over one more time and try again, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: モロヘイヤさんは、 野球をやったことがないのです！
English: Mr. Wellington has never played baseball!

Japanese: だから、グローブというものを 知らなかった！
English: That would explain why he didn't even know what a glove is!

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Uh, Nick...

Japanese: こおりついちゃったじゃない！ ‥‥空気が！
English: I hope you've noticed the icy glares we're getting from everyone in here!

Japanese: そ‥‥それがいったい、 どうだと言うんですか？
English: Wh-What in the world do you mean?

Japanese: 中学時代、ボクはピッチャーで ４番だったけどね。
English: When I was in junior high, I was a star pitcher... Well, 4th in line, actually, but...

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Whoops...

Japanese: もう一度。 考えなおしてください。
English: Think it over one more time and try again, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥ところで、モロヘイヤさん。 視力はいくつですか？
English: By the way, just how bad are your eyes?

Japanese: な‥‥。なな、ななな、 ななななんだい急に！
English: Huh? How... What... You... Why are you asking me about this all of a sudden!?

Japanese: バナナとグローブを見まちがえた ぐらいで、視力が低いとは‥‥
English: Your Honor, it is very simple to mistake a glove for a bunch of bananas...

Japanese: ふつう、まちがえません。 異議は却下します。
English: No, I don't think so. Objection overruled.

Japanese: ヤ‥‥ヤレヤレ。 あなたがたは、アレだ。
English: Y-Y-You... You're one of those people. Yes, you know what I mean.

Japanese: 地動説を唱えたガリレオを 認めなかった連中といっしょだ。
English: You're like those people who refused to accept Galileo for his Copernican Theory!

Japanese: くだらんジョーシキにしばられて 新しい可能性に気づきもしない！
English: You're too used to your world view to realize that there are other, new possibilities!

Japanese: たしかに、結果的にアレは グローブだったのかもしれない。
English: Sure, in the end, we find out that it is in fact, a glove, not bananas. However...

Japanese: しかし、ハナからそれを 疑ってかかるなんて‥‥
English: when viewed from afar, I do think there is room enough for doubt, don't you...?

Japanese: だから、いくつなんですか視力は！
English: And that is why I asked you how bad your eyesight is!

Japanese: 両方とも０．１だよ！　ワルいか！
English: They're both 20/200. I suppose you're going to tell me that's terrible, right!?

Japanese: 今日はどうしてメガネを‥‥？
English: Why are you not wearing your glasses today then?

Japanese: ‥‥さ、最近ちょっと。 その、なくしてしまって‥‥。
English: Ummm... That's because I lost them recently, you see...

Japanese: も、モチロン作りなおそうと 思ってますよ！
English: Of course, I was planning on getting a new pair made right away!

Japanese: でも、なかなかボクの おメガネにかなうメガネが‥‥
English: But you know, my glasses are no ordinary glasses, so to replace them--

Japanese: 事件を目撃したときは？ メガネをかけていましたか？
English: How about when you witnessed the crime? Were you wearing your glasses then?

Japanese: どうなんですか！
English: How about it, witness!?

Japanese: ‥‥あ、あなたがたはアレだ。
English: Y-You are an unrelenting, evil man.

Japanese: あのジャンヌダルクを処刑した 連中といっしょだ。
English: You're like those people who rejected Joan of Arc and put her to death!

Japanese: 勇気を持って、正しいおこないを した者をつかまえて、
English: She was brave and courageous, only to be caught by horrible, unrighteous people.

Japanese: なんにも悪くないのに 火あぶりに‥‥
English: And while she didn't do anything wrong, she was still gruesomely burned at the--

Japanese: ‥‥つまり 証人はメガネをかけていなかった！
English: Which boils down to you were not wearing your glasses at that time!

Japanese: それならば！
English: Therefore!

Japanese: 目撃したという“女”が 被告だったのかどうか‥‥、
English: The identity of the "woman" at the scene of the crime and that of the defendant

Japanese: この証人に断言できるはずがない！
English: can not be proven to be the same by this witness!

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、 現場の段差は、たった３ｍです！
English: But the height difference was only 9 feet!

Japanese: 上の遊歩道にいる被告のカオが 見えても、おかしくはない！
English: It was very possible for him to see the face of the culprit standing on the upper path!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥証人。
English: Hmm... Witness.

Japanese: 証言は正確におねがいします。 人の命がかかっているのですよ。
English: Please be more accurate in your testimony. Remember, a person's life is at stake.

Japanese: ‥‥‥わ、わかりましたよ！
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor!

Japanese: では、証言をつづけてください。
English: Now then, please continue with your testimony.

Japanese: では、事件を目撃してからのことを 証言してもらいましょう。
English: Please tell the court what happened next, in the moments after you witnessed the crime.

Japanese: ～事件を目撃してからのこと～
English: -- What Happened Next --

Japanese: 遊歩道の女は、ボクに気づくと サッと逃げていきましたね。
English: The girl on the upper path ran away as soon as she realized I was there.

Japanese: そのあと、ボクはすぐに警察に 通報しました。
English: After that, I immediately called the police station to report the crime.

Japanese: 警察に電話が入ったのは、 ６時４５分だったみたいですね。
English: It must've been 6:45 PM when I made the call.

Japanese: きっとヒマだったのでしょう。 １０分もしないで駆けつけてきた。
English: They must have a lot of free time on their hands since they showed up within 10 minutes.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 遊歩道の人物は、あなたを見て 逃げだしたワケですね。
English: So the person who was on the upper path saw you and then ran away.

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: Yes, that is correct.

Japanese: ボクほどアタマがよくなくても、 さすがにリカイできるでしょう。
English: Which is why, even someone without a superior brain like mine can understand that...

Japanese: ‥‥この女こそが犯人だ、とね！
English: that girl is the murderer!

Japanese: では、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: You may question the witness now, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ～事件を目撃してからのこと～
English: -- What Happened Next --

Japanese: 遊歩道の女は、ボクに気づくと サッと逃げていきましたね。
English: The girl on the upper path ran away as soon as she realized I was there.

Japanese: そのあと、ボクはすぐに警察に 通報しました。
English: After that, I immediately called the police station to report the crime.

Japanese: 警察に電話が入ったのは、 ６時４５分だったみたいですね。
English: It must've been 6:45 PM when I made the call.

Japanese: きっとヒマだったのでしょう。 １０分もしないで駆けつけてきた。
English: They must have a lot of free time on their hands since they showed up within 10 minutes.

Japanese: （今の証言‥‥。おかしいところ、 あるかなあ‥‥）
English: (I can't find anything out of the ordinary in his testimony...)

Japanese: もう一度、見てみようよ。 ‥‥法廷記録。
English: Why don't you take one more look at the Court Record?

Japanese: そうだね。
English: Yeah, I guess I should.

Japanese: すぐに逃げていったんですか？
English: She ran away, just like that?

Japanese: そう。まさに“たつ鳥、 あとをにごさず”‥‥みたいな。
English: Yes, she did. She saw me and flew the nest like the guilty bird she is.

Japanese: ‥‥おっと！　三流大学出身の キミには、ムズかしい表現かな。
English: Oh, I'm sorry. Was that pun too hard for someone who only got a third-rate education?

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ぼくには リカイできない表現だな）
English: (Actually, that did take me a few seconds to get...)

Japanese: ところで、すぐに逃げたのに それが被告とわかったんですか？
English: Anyway, if she ran away the instant she saw you, how could you tell it was my client?
Eek!

Japanese: 証人は、すでにその質問に 答えています！
English: The witness has already answered that question.

Japanese: “その女は被告だった”と！
English: He has stated that the defendant is the culprit!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 弁護人の質問を却下します。
English: This is true. Mr. Wright, I'm striking your question from the record.

Japanese: （どうも引っかかるな‥‥）
English: (Hmm, how can I get more information out of him?)

Japanese: “すぐ”‥‥というのは？
English: Immediately? As in?

Japanese: だから“すぐ”だよ。 １分もたたなかったと思うね！
English: As in immediately! I mean, sure, a minute might have elapsed before I did, but...

Japanese: それが市民のギム、ってヤツさ。 ‥‥三流大学で習わなかったかい？
English: That's the duty of every good citizen, or did they not teach that at your pitiful school?

Japanese: （そんなの、 大学で教わることかよ！）
English: (You think people learn about how to call the police in COLLEGE!?)

Japanese: ね、ね、なるほどくん。 法廷記録、ちょっと見てみて。
English: Hey, Nick. I think you should take a look at the Court Record for a sec.

Japanese: どうしてその時間を‥‥？
English: How do you know what time it was...?

Japanese: あの刑事が教えてくれたんだよ。
English: That detective told me. You know which one I mean.

Japanese: ホラ。あの、いかにも三流中学出身 っぽいコートを着た‥‥
English: The one with the jacket that makes him look like a dropout from a no-name high school.

Japanese: 自分はちゃんと二流‥‥ イヤ一流大学を出てるッス！
English: Hey, pal! I graduated from a pretty good, I mean, top-ranked college!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (I don't believe this.)

Japanese: とにかく、時間はまちがいないと 思うよ。
English: It doesn't matter. I don't believe I was mistaken on what time I called.

Japanese: ちがってるとしたら、あの刑事が 時計の読み方を知らなかったのさ。
English: And if I am wrong, then that detective obviously doesn't know how to tell time.

Japanese: なんスとお‥‥！　自分は‥‥ 自分は二流、イヤ一流の‥‥
English: What!? Why you!!! You're just some lousy kid who...

Japanese: なるほど。‥‥それで、 通報を受けた警察は‥‥？
English: I think the court can see your point. Anyway, how did the police respond?

Japanese: ということは、７時にはもう 警察が到着していた‥‥？ 7:00 PM?
English: So you're saying that there were police on the scene by

Japanese: もっと早かったと思うよ。
English: They got there before that, I think.

Japanese: あのあたりは、あまりヒトが 通らないんだけどね。
English: There usually aren't many people in that area at that time of day.

Japanese: アッという間にヤジウマで イッパイさ。
English: But suddenly, before I knew it, there were people crawling all over gawking.

Japanese: ‥‥この国のモラルの低さには 絶望するよ‥‥。
English: It certainly says something about the morals of the people in this country.

Japanese: ‥‥これを見てください。
English: Mr. Wellington, would you please take a look at this?

Japanese: 被害者の解剖記録‥‥ですか？
English: You mean the victim's autopsy report?

Japanese: 記録によると、事件があったのは、 ６時２８分です。
English: According to this, the murder occurred at 6:28 PM.

Japanese: それがどうかしたかい？
English: So what of it?

Japanese: あなたは、その瞬間を目撃した。 それからすぐに警察に通報した。
English: You said that you called the police immediately after the murder took place.

Japanese: しかし、警察に電話が入ったのは ６時４５分です。
English: However, by the time you had called the police, it was already 6:45 PM.

Japanese: 事件発生から１５分も たっているじゃないですか！
English: There is clearly a 15 minute gap here! Do you deny it!?
Aaaack!

Japanese: この空白の１５分間‥‥。 あなたは何をしていたのか！
English: I think this court would like to hear what you were doing during this 15 minute gap!

Japanese: ぐぅぅぅぅッ！
English: Grrrrrr!

Japanese: 証人は、死体を見て 動転していたのです！
English: The witness was in shock at the time after witnessing a terrible murder!

Japanese: ちょっぴり、放心していた のではないでしょうか‥‥。
English: It's only to be expected that he would be a little dazed...

Japanese: １５分を“ちょっぴり”とは 言わない！
English: Fifteen minutes is hardly what I would call "a little dazed"!

Japanese: ぎゃうッ！
English: Aaaaah!

Japanese: 証人！
English: Mr. Wellington.

Japanese: うッ‥‥！
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: ‥‥警察に通報するまで、 あなたは何をしていたのですか！
English: Explain yourself. What were you doing during those 15 minutes?

Japanese: 答えてください！
English: Answer the question!

Japanese: ‥‥でんわ‥‥
English: ... I... Uhh... Telephone... Err... I mean...

Japanese: なんですって？
English: Spit it out!

Japanese: ‥‥公衆電話を探していたんだよ！
English: I... I was searching for a phone booth!

Japanese: 公衆電話‥‥
English: A phone booth?

Japanese: あの。証人は 携帯電話はお持ちでは‥‥？
English: You mean, you don't have a cell phone?

Japanese: そ、その、重箱のスミをつつく ような、キタナイやりかた‥‥
English: You and your questions! As if you're trying to open all the layers of a Matryoshka doll.

Japanese: イカガなものかと思いますがね！
English: You must think you're really something special!

Japanese: ‥‥証人ッ！
English: Witness!

Japanese: 落としちゃったんだよ！ 携帯電話を！
English: I-I lost my cell phone! There! Are you happy!?

Japanese: 落とした‥‥？
English: You lost it...?

Japanese: ‥‥まったく、メガネはなくすは、 携帯電話は落とすは‥‥。
English: Unbelievable! You lose your glasses, and your cell phone!

Japanese: よくモノをなくすヒトですね。
English: You must be very scatterbrained when it comes to your belongings.

Japanese: なんだ！　一流のニンゲンは 落とし物なんかしないってか！
English: What!? Are you saying that first-rate people are never allowed to lose things!?

Japanese: いいか。天才と呼ばれる連中は、 みんなどこかがオカシイんだよ。
English: Haven't you ever heard that all geniuses have a strange quirk or two?

Japanese: 逆に言えば、オカシイところが あるボクは天才なんだ。
English: So by that rationale, since I have my own quirk, it would mean that I am a genius!

Japanese: まあ、キミたち凡人には とうていリカイが‥‥
English: I don't think simple, plain people like you can underst--

Japanese: ‥‥そこまでッ！
English: Enough!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Oh man, oh man...)

Japanese: （‥‥って、待てよ。 携帯電話を‥‥落とした‥‥？）
English: (Wait! Hold on a second. He lost... his cell phone?)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ その携帯電話って、もしかして！
English: Nick! That cell phone! Could it be...?

Japanese: この、マコちゃんが拾ったヤツ だってのか？　まさか‥‥！
English: You mean this phone Maggey found? There's no way...!

Japanese: （とんでもないことになってきた！ ‥‥どうする？）
English: (Boy, I didn't see this coming. What should I do now...?)

Japanese: （‥‥まさかな。この電話が あいつのものだなんて）
English: (It's probably just a coincidence.)

Japanese: （そんなグーゼン、あるわけ‥‥）
English: (I mean, what are the chances that this phone is that snob's anyway?)

Japanese: ダ、ダメだよなるほどくん！
English: Nick!? What do you think you're doing!?

Japanese: これはちゃんと たしかめておかなきゃ！
English: You really should check out this lead!

Japanese: どうしましたか？　弁護人。
English: Is there a problem, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: は、はい！ （もう一度、考えなおすか‥‥）
English: N-No, Your Honor. (I should give this some more thought...)

Japanese: モロヘイヤさん！
English: Mr. Wellington!

Japanese: なくした携帯電話は、 今どこに‥‥？
English: Where is your cell phone right now?

Japanese: ヤレヤレ‥‥。 何をあわてているんだか。
English: Heh, what are you getting all excited about? You seem to be a little confused.

Japanese: ちゃんと見つけたよ。 ホラ、これだ。
English: I found my phone, I'll have you know. See. Here is it.

Japanese: あ‥‥、そうなんですか。
English: Oh... I see...

Japanese: なんだ、ちゃんと持ってるよ。
English: Hmm, looks like he's got his phone.

Japanese: ‥‥あたしてっきり、それ、 あの人のモノだと思ったのに。
English: And I thought that just maybe this was his.

Japanese: うーん‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Well then, I think we've cleared this issue up.

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき、証人は 携帯電話を落としていた。
English: At the time of the murder, the witness did not have his cell phone because he had lost it.

Japanese: だから公衆電話を探していて、 通報がおくれた、と。
English: Therefore, the delay in his call was caused by his search for a phone booth.

Japanese: まあ、おおむねそのとおりと 考えても、特に問題はないですね。
English: Well, that's the gist of it. I guess you could put it that way and leave it at that.

Japanese: どうですか？　弁護人。 これで問題ありませんね？
English: Do you have any further questions, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。 問題ないでしょう。
English: Hmm... No, I think I'm done here, Your Honor.

Japanese: ちょっとちょっと！ ‥‥どうしちゃったの今日は！
English: Wait, wait, wait! What is with you today, Nick!?

Japanese: ちゃんと法廷記録、見てよー！
English: Take a good look at the Court Record!!
Huh?
What are you talking...? Ah... Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: どうしましたか？ やっぱり、問題があると？
English: What is it, Mr. Wright? By your screaming, I assume you have a question after all?

Japanese: 裁判長！ まったく、お話になりません。
English: Your Honor! The witness' testimony does not make sense!

Japanese: モロヘイヤさんが、公衆電話を 探していたはずがない！
English: I don't believe that there was ever a need for the witness to search for a phone!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって！
English: H-How dare you!

Japanese: そこまでキメつけるからには 当然、証拠があるんでしょうな！
English: You can't just make outrageous claims like that! You do have some sort of proof, don't you?

Japanese: え！　‥‥も、もちろんです。 （だいじょうぶだよな、証拠‥‥）
English: Well, yeah... O-Of course! (This evidence should be good enough, I think...)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 では、示してもらいましょう。
English: Alright. Let's have this proof, then.

Japanese: 証人が、公衆電話を探していた はずがないという証拠を！
English: Please present proof that the witness had no need to search for a public phone booth!

Japanese: カンタンなことですよ。 ‥‥証拠は、これです。
English: It's very simple! This is the evidence that backs up my claim!

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらず、自信たっぷりに 意味不明なモノを持ち出しますね。
English: And yet again you have presented this court with an obscure, meaningless item.

Japanese: え？　意味不明ですか？ ‥‥この証拠品。
English: Huh? This evidence? It's meaningless?

Japanese: わかんないよ！
English: I don't get it at all!

Japanese: そもそも、どうしてあのヒトが 公衆電話を探すハズがないの？
English: Why do you think he wouldn't need to look for a phone in the first place!?

Japanese: え。‥‥どうして、って‥‥。
English: Well, that's... because...

Japanese: なんか、証拠品を見ていて 思ったんだよ。
English: I was looking at the evidence and it just hit me.

Japanese: “公衆電話なら 探すまでもないぞ”って。
English: I thought, "Hey, he really didn't have to look for a phone."

Japanese: じゃあ、じゃあ、その証拠品を 提示しようよ！
English: Then why don't you hurry up and present that piece of evidence!?

Japanese: （あれは‥‥ どの証拠品だったっけ‥‥）
English: (Hmm, now what was that piece of evidence again...?)

Japanese: 考えるまでもありません。 ‥‥これを見てください。
English: It's quite simple, actually. Please take a look at this.

Japanese: 事件の現場写真、ですか？
English: At the crime scene photo?

Japanese: それがどうかしたかね？
English: Is there a problem with it?

Japanese: “どうかしたかね” じゃありませんよ。
English: Oh, there's nothing wrong with the picture.

Japanese: これを見てもわからないなんて、 それこそどうかしている！
English: But if you don't understand my logic after looking at it, something is wrong with you!

Japanese: うおおおッ！
English: Noooo!

Japanese: で‥‥電話ボックス‥‥！
English: It's... It's... A phone booth!

Japanese: そのとおり！
English: That is correct!

Japanese: 証人は、３歩あるくだけで 電話ボックスにたどりつけた！
English: All the defendant had to do was walk three steps!

Japanese: 証人！　‥‥なぜ、 そうしなかったのですか！
English: Mr. Wellington! Why did you not use the phone that was right in front of you!?

Japanese: ぐはああぁッ！
English: Ooooooougn!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: つ、通報がおくれたから なんだと言うのですか！
English: Wh-What does reporting the crime a little late prove for you!?

Japanese: 証人は、その１５分間の ことを説明できないでいる！
English: The witness can't explain what he was doing for those 15 minutes!

Japanese: これはかなり、アヤシイ！
English: That is reason enough to throw suspicion on his testimony!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥たしかに。 どうなんですか、証人！
English: Yes, this is very true. What do you have to say for yourself, witness!?

Japanese: ね。ね。やっぱり、その携帯電話、 アイツのなんじゃないかな。
English: Then I bet this phone really is his, Nick!

Japanese: それを取りもどすために 殺したんだよ！　町尾巡査。
English: He must've killed Dustin to get his phone back!

Japanese: でも、マコちゃんは、アイツに それを返すはずだったんだぞ。
English: But Maggey said that she was going to return it to him.

Japanese: 殺す必要なんて、ないよ。
English: So there was no reason for him to kill for it.

Japanese: それに、マコちゃんが拾った電話、 そもそもまだ、ここにあるし。
English: And on top of that, we still have the phone she found anyway.

Japanese: うーん‥‥。 でも、携帯電話じゃないとしたら、
English: Hmm... But if he wasn't looking for his cell phone,

Japanese: 何を探してたのかな？ モロヘイヤさん。
English: was he looking for something else...?

Japanese: （何かを探していた、か‥‥）
English: (Was he...?)

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: あなたには、何か考えが あるのではないですか？
English: Do you have any thoughts you would like to share with the court?

Japanese: 空白の１５分のあいだ、 この証人が何をしていたか‥‥？
English: Can you offer an explanation as to what the witness was doing during those 15 minutes?

Japanese: 今の段階ではまだ、なんとも 言えないですね‥‥。
English: I'm afraid it's too early to say anything for sure at this time.

Japanese: そうですか‥‥。 亜内検事。
English: I see... Mr. Payne?

Japanese: 他に証言してもらうことは ありますか？
English: Will we be hearing further testimony from the prosecution?

Japanese: いいえ。以上です。
English: No, Your Honor. That is all.

Japanese: え！
English: Oh crud...

Japanese: この証人は、犯行現場で “被告を見た”と証言しています。
English: This witness has said that he saw the defendant commit the crime at the crime scene.

Japanese: たしかにそのとき、証人は メガネをかけていませんでした。
English: While it is true that the witness was not wearing his glasses at the time,

Japanese: しかし、犯人との距離は近かった。 それを目撃することは可能でした。
English: the court feels that since he was near the culprit, positive identification is possible.

Japanese: ということは‥‥？
English: Which means...?

Japanese: 被告人・須々木　マコの犯行は、 じゅうぶん立証されたと考えます。
English: The guilt of the defendant, Maggey Byrde, has been sufficiently substantiated.

Japanese: えええええええっ！
English: WHAAAAAAAT!?

Japanese: なるほどくん！ なんとかしないと‥‥！
English: Nick! You have to do something before it's too late!!

Japanese: それでは、これで尋問を終了‥‥
English: I hereby close the cross-exa--

Japanese: わ、わかりました！
English: W-Wait!

Japanese: モロヘイヤさんが、空白の １５分間に何をしていたか？
English: As to the actions of Mr. Wellington during the 15 minute gap,

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側の考えを 述べたいと思います！
English: the defense would like to propose an explanation!

Japanese: さきほど弁護人は、それはムリだと 認めたばかりですぞ！
English: I thought the defense had just finished proving that this couldn't be explained!

Japanese: １つだけ、可能性があります！
English: No, there is one possibility!

Japanese: わかりました。 弁護人の考えを聞きましょう。
English: Alright. Let's hear your explanation.

Japanese: ‥‥ただし。その考えに説得力が なかった場合‥‥
English: However, be forewarned that if your explanation is not persuasive,

Japanese: ペナルティを与えます。 ‥‥よく考えてください。
English: you will be penalized. Think carefully before you present, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Yes, Your Honor!

Japanese: （しまった‥‥。“１つだけ” なんて言わなきゃよかった‥‥）
English: (Urk. I probably shouldn't have said there was only one possibility...)

Japanese: 以下の疑問の答えを示す 証拠品を提示してください。
English: Please present to the court the one piece of evidence that will answer the following:

Japanese: “証人はなぜ、警察にすぐ 通報しなかったか‥‥？”
English: "Why didn't the witness call the police right away?"

Japanese: その証拠は、あるいは コレではないでしょうか！
English: Perhaps this is the evidence you need to be convinced!

Japanese: “あるいは”、ですね？
English: Perhaps?

Japanese: そ、そうですよ。 単なる一例ですから、今のは。
English: Aha ha ha. That was just an idea I thought I'd throw out.

Japanese: 別の“あるいは”を 見せてください。
English: I suggest that "perhaps" you should find a better piece of evidence.

Japanese: は、はい！　では‥‥
English: Yes, of course, Your Honor! Well then...

Japanese: その前に！ ペナルティを与えます。
English: But before you do, you will be penalized.

Japanese: （いたたた‥‥）
English: (Ouch.)

Japanese: ‥‥１つだけ、考えがあります。
English: There is only one possible explanation.

Japanese: モロヘイヤさん！
English: Mr. Wellington!

Japanese: な、なんだ！ オドかさないでくれたまえ‥‥。
English: Wh-What!? Don't do that! You almost made me have a heart attack!

Japanese: これは、あなたのメガネですね？
English: These are your glasses, aren't they?

Japanese: あっ！　ど、どこでそれを‥‥ ハッ‥‥‥！
English: Ah! Where... Where did you find--!? Ghaaaa!

Japanese: ‥‥聞きましたかみなさん。 彼は今、認めましたね。
English: I believe the court all heard what you just confessed to:

Japanese: このメガネが、 自分のものであることを！
English: That these glasses are in fact yours!

Japanese: モロヘイヤさん、 教えてあげましょう。
English: I'll tell you where they were found, Mr. Wellington.

Japanese: こいつは、被害者の死体の下から 発見されたんですよ。
English: These glasses were found under the victim's body.

Japanese: ななな、なんだってェェェ！ 死体の下ァァァ！
English: U-Under the v-victim's body!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: まま、ま、ま、待ってくれ！
English: N-Now, w-wait a second! Hold on!

Japanese: ボ、ボクは、 何も認めたワケじゃないぞ！
English: I-I didn't confess or confirm a-any-anything!

Japanese: 裁判長！ 答えはハッキリしています！
English: Your Honor! I think the answer is quite clear here!

Japanese: 町尾巡査は、つき落とされたとき、 犯人のメガネをつかみとった！
English: As he fell, Dustin Prince grabbed the culprit's glasses.

Japanese: メガネをなくした犯人は、 必死でそれを探した！
English: The culprit knew that he had to find his glasses, and searched frantically for them.

Japanese: しかし、死体の下には 気がつかなかった！
English: What he didn't realize was that they were under the victim's body!

Japanese: ‥‥だから、通報が１５分、 おくれたのです！
English: And that is why it took him 15 minutes to make that call!

Japanese: べ‥‥弁護人！ つ、つまりあなたは‥‥
English: M-Mr. Wright! Are you...?

Japanese: 告発するつもりですか！ この証人を‥‥真犯人だと！
English: Are you indicting the witness as the real murderer!?

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、そのつもりです！
English: Of course! That is precisely what I am doing!

Japanese: う‥‥‥‥ うおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！！
English: Oooo...OOOOWAAAAAAAAAA AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAH!

Japanese: （まちがいない！）
English: (I know I'm right!)

Japanese: （真犯人は、アイツだ！）
English: (He is the real murderer!)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ゼンブ、わかっちゃったの？
English: Did you figure it out, Nick!?

Japanese: ‥‥なんとなく、ね。
English: More or less.

Japanese: やっぱり、事件のカギは この携帯電話だったんだよ。
English: Turns out this cell phone was the key to this case after all.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、 アイツを沈めるチャンスだ！
English: Anyway, now is our chance to deep-six this guy.

Japanese: イッキに追いつめるぞ！
English: I'll sink him in one shot!

Japanese: う‥‥うん！
English: Yeah!

Japanese: なんだかあたし、コーフンして きちゃったよ‥‥！
English: This is so exciting, watching you work again!

Japanese: （‥‥どうやらぼくは 自分をとりもどしつつあった）
English: (Somehow, my old self is coming back to me.)

Japanese: （ノルかソルかの勝負。 その、身を切るような一瞬！）
English: (It's time to sink or swim; everything rests on the edge of a knife!)

Japanese: （それはたしかに、ぼくがいつも 求めていたものだった‥‥）
English: (This is the moment I've been waiting for...)

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 裁判長！ べ‥‥弁護人に法廷ブジョク罪を！
English: Your Honor! The defense... The defense is making a mockery of this court!

Japanese: なんのコンキョもなく 証人を犯人呼ばわりして‥‥
English: Without any solid ground to stand on, he accuses the witness of being the murderer!

Japanese: そそ、そ、そうだ！
English: Y-Y-Yeah! That-That's right!

Japanese: ボ、ボクが犯人なワケ、 ないじゃないかッ！
English: I... I'm no criminal!

Japanese: この‥‥この、三流の ヘボ弁護士が‥‥
English: Th-This third-rate, fraud of a lawyer...

Japanese: ‥‥モロヘイヤさん。 じゃあ、逆に聞きましょう。
English: In that case, why don't we look at it from a different perspective.

Japanese: どうしてあなたが 犯人じゃないと言えるんですか！
English: Let's hear your explanation as to why you are NOT the murderer!

Japanese: な‥‥！　そんな、 カンタンなコトだろ！
English: Why, that's... That's easy... Um... Uh...

Japanese: たとえば、アレがある！ ‥‥被害者が書き残した名前！
English: For example... There's um... The name the victim wrote! What about that...?

Japanese: ああ。‥‥あの《鈴木》という 文字のことですか？
English: Oh, you mean the name "Maggie"?

Japanese: い、いくらキミでも、 それぐらいは読めるだろう？
English: Y-Yeah! Even an idiot like you can read that, right?

Japanese: しかしこれは、被害者が 書いたものではない！
English: But we already know this was not written by the victim himself.

Japanese: 被告人の名前は《須々木》だし、 被害者は左ききでした。
English: After all, the defendant's name is "Maggey" and the victim was left-handed.

Japanese: つまりこれは、被告人に 罪を着せるために、
English: So basically, you are saying that in order to make the defendant look guilty,

Japanese: 真犯人が被害者の右手を使って 書き残した、ということですね？
English: the real criminal used the victim's right hand to write her name on the ground?

Japanese: で、でも！　でもでも！
English: B-But... But, but!!

Japanese: 真犯人は、被告の 知り合いってコトだろう？
English: Wouldn't that mean that the real criminal was someone the defendant knew?

Japanese: 彼女の名前が“スズキ”だって 知っていたわけだからな！
English: Otherwise, how else would that person know her name was "Maggie", er "Maggey"!?

Japanese: たしかに、そのとおりです。
English: That is a good point.

Japanese: この証人は、須々木　マコさんを 知らないはずですね。
English: The witness didn't even know of Ms. Byrde before this trial.

Japanese: （どうなんだ‥‥！）
English: (Ah, I forgot!)

Japanese: （アイツに、被告人の名前を 知るチャンスはなかったのか？）
English: (Hmm, was there any way this creep could've known Maggey's name beforehand?)

Japanese: （ダメだ！ どう考えたって‥‥）
English: (No matter how I look at it, it's no good!)

Japanese: （アイツがマコちゃんのことを 知っていたはずがない！）
English: (There is no way he could have known Maggey or her name!)

Japanese: ダ、ダメだよなるほどくん！ アイツに逃げられちゃう！
English: Nick! You can't let this slimebag get away!

Japanese: もっと、考えないと！
English: Think harder!

Japanese: そ、それはそうだけど‥‥ （とにかく、整理してみるか）
English: Y-You're right... (OK, let's go over this from the beginning one more time.)

Japanese: （ようするに、証人にチャンスが あったことを証明すればいい）
English: (It would be best if I could prove that the witness had a chance to learn...)

Japanese: （彼女の名前が“スズキ”だと 知るチャンスが‥‥）
English: (that the defendant's name was "Maggey".)

Japanese: では、弁護人。 提示していただきましょうか。
English: Now, will the defense please present its case?

Japanese: この証人が、どうやって 被告の名前を知ることができたか？
English: How could the witness have known the defendant's name?

Japanese: その証拠は、これです！
English: This is how!

Japanese: 弁護人が何もわかっていない という証拠ですかな？
English: Am I supposed to take this as evidence that the defense doesn't know what's going on?

Japanese: あ、あれ？　‥‥ははは。
English: Huh?... Oh... Ah ha ha ha.

Japanese: もう、笑ってすまされるような トシではないですぞ！
English: Aren't you a little old to be laughing like a 5-year old? Take some responsibility!

Japanese: （あんたにトシのコトを 言われたくないぞ‥‥）
English: (As if I need a lecture about responsibility from you, of all people...)

Japanese: 弁護人にペナルティを与えます。
English: The defense will receive a penalty.

Japanese: もう一度、考えなおしてください。
English: Please think carefully before presenting your case again.

Japanese: はい‥‥。
English: Yes, Your Honor...

Japanese: ‥‥モロヘイヤさん。
English: Mr. Wellington.

Japanese: 事件があったとき、携帯電話を 落としていたそうですね？
English: You didn't have your cell phone with you on the day of the murder, correct?

Japanese: そ、それがどうかしたかい！
English: So what if I didn't?

Japanese: それに気づいたあなたは、 どうしましたか？
English: When you realized you had lost it, what did you do?

Japanese: ‥‥ど、どうした、って‥‥？
English: What did I do?

Japanese: 落とした携帯電話の番号に、 かけてみませんでしたか？
English: Didn't you try to find it by calling it?

Japanese: ‥‥！　ど、どうしてそれを‥‥
English: Why you...! How did you...!?

Japanese: 裁判長！　今の質問は、事件に なんら関係が‥‥
English: Your Honor! These questions have nothing to do with...

Japanese: 却下。
English: Overruled.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。どういうことですか？
English: Mr. Wright, where are you going with this line of questioning?

Japanese: この証人の携帯電話が、 事件に関係あるのですか？
English: Do you think there is some relation between this witness' cell phone and the murder?

Japanese: ‥‥あります。
English: I do, Your Honor.

Japanese: 須々木　マコは事件当日、公園で 携帯電話を拾っています。
English: On the day of the murder, Maggey Byrde picked up a lost phone in the park.

Japanese: ‥‥そして！
English: And!

Japanese: 落とし主からの電話を一度、 受けているのです！
English: She also received a phone call from the owner of the phone!

Japanese: 『あ、あの、もしもし。 スズキと申しますが‥‥』
English: "Um, hello?"

Japanese: 『あ。すみません。その携帯電話を 落とした者なんですけど‥‥』
English: "Oh, thank you! I've been searching for my phone."

Japanese: 『よかった！　じゃァさっそく お会いして、お返ししますッ！』
English: "Is this yours? Oh, I'm glad you called! We can meet up and I can give this back!"

Japanese: 『すぐ、そちらにうかがいます。 もう一度、お名前を‥‥』
English: "I'll be right there, um... I'm sorry, I didn't catch your name..."

Japanese: 『はッ！　スズキ　マコッス！』
English: "You can call me Maggey!"

Japanese: あなたは、その時に知ったのです。 彼女の名前が“スズキ”だと！
English: That was when you learned that her name was "Maggey"!

Japanese: う‥‥ぐぐ‥‥
English: Uh, um, nnngh...

Japanese: しかし、あなたは１つ カンちがいをした。
English: But you made one fatal mistake.

Japanese: カンちがい‥‥？
English: Fatal mistake?

Japanese: 被告の名前は《須々木》です。 書き残された名前は《鈴木》‥‥
English: My client's name is "Maggey" but the name that was written on the ground was "Maggie".

Japanese: このまちがいは、耳で聞いたとき にしか、起こらない！
English: This is a mistake that could only occur if all you knew was how her name sounded!

Japanese: ひいイィッ！
English: EEEEEEEEEK!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: お、おま‥‥お待ちください！
English: B-But, Your Honor!

Japanese: この証人には、動機がありません！
English: The witness has no motive!

Japanese: ほほお。‥‥と、言いますと？
English: And your point is?

Japanese: カンタンなことです。 人を殺すには、何か理由がある。
English: It's very simple, Your Honor. A person usually would not kill someone without a reason.

Japanese: モロヘイヤくんには、 その理由がありません！
English: Mr. Wellington had no reason to kill anyone!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうともっ！ ドーキがないよボクには！
English: That is absolutely correct! I don't have a motive!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥。弁護人。
English: Hmm... Mr. Wright.

Japanese: はい。
English: Your Honor?

Japanese: 証明できますかな？ この証人の“動機”を‥‥。
English: Can you explain what motive this witness could have had?

Japanese: カンタンなコトです。
English: It's very simple, Your Honor.

Japanese: だだ、だいじょうぶなの？ なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Are you sure, Nick!?

Japanese: だって、“できません”て言ったら 終わっちゃうだろ？　裁判。
English: If I said I can't offer an explanation, then the trial's over, right?

Japanese: そ、そりゃそうだけど‥‥。
English: Yeah, but...

Japanese: では、提示してもらいましょう。 この証人の“動機”を示す証拠を！
English: Now then, please present to this court proof that the witness had a motive!

Japanese: 犯人は、これが理由で 被害者を殺したのではないか！
English: The murderer killed the victim because of this!

Japanese: 弁護人は、苦しまぎれに デタラメな証拠を提出しておる！
English: The defense is obviously haphazardly throwing out evidence in desperation!

Japanese: そのようですね‥‥。
English: It certainly seems that way.

Japanese: ここまできて、 ハッタリは通用しませんぞ！
English: Don't let all your hard work up to this point go to waste on a random guess.

Japanese: ‥‥しまった‥‥。
English: What was I thinking...?

Japanese: モロヘイヤの動機はコイツです！
English: Mr. Wellington's motive is right here!

Japanese: 携帯電話‥‥。
English: The cell phone?

Japanese: 須々木　マコが拾った電話には、 ある電話番号が登録されていた！
English: In the memory of the phone the defendant found was a list of certain phone numbers!

Japanese: モロヘイヤの動機はコイツです！
English: Mr. Wellington's motive is right here!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか、 それは‥‥リスト？
English: What is this? ...A list?

Japanese: 須々木　マコが拾った携帯電話に 登録されていた電話番号です。
English: These phone numbers were pulled from the memory of the phone the defendant found.

Japanese: これらの名前は、あるグループの メンバーだとわかりました！
English: And we have determined that the people on this list are members of a "certain group".

Japanese: し‥‥調べたのか？ そ、その番号を‥‥。
English: You... You looked up all those numbers...?

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん。
English: Of course.

Japanese: これは、その電話に登録されていた 番号のリストです。
English: This list of phone numbers was stored in the cell phone's memory.

Japanese: 電話番号とその名前はすべて、ある 詐欺グループのメンバーのものだ！
English: The names and numbers belong to people who are members of a certain con artists' group.

Japanese: ななな、なんですってぇ！ さ‥‥詐欺ぐるーぷ‥‥？
English: Wh-Wh-Wh-Wh-What!? C-C-Con artists!?

Japanese: どうして、そんな番号が 登録されているのですか！
English: Can you explain why these numbers were on your phone, Mr. Wellington!?

Japanese: こ、こ、これは犯罪だ！
English: Th-This... This is an outrage! An invasion of privacy!

Japanese: ヒトの電話の番号を調べるなんて、 殺人以上にヒレツな‥‥
English: Looking up the phone numbers on a person's phone is a worse crime than murder!

Japanese: あ、あなたがたはアレだ。
English: Y-You're one of those people!

Japanese: ユトリロのアトリエに踏みこんだ 警官たちといっしょだ！
English: You're just like the cops who raided that brilliant artist, Maurice Utrillo's atelier!

Japanese: 天才のシゴトを踏みにじり、 美の女神との語らいを
English: They disrupted a genius at work and interrupted his dialogue with the Goddess of--

Japanese: そんなことはどうでもいい！
English: I don't care, Mr. Wellington!

Japanese: このリストについて 説明していただきたい！
English: All I want is for you to tell us what this list is about!

Japanese: キサマらに、一流のニンゲンの 気持ちなんてわかるかァッ！
English: Do you think you -- any of you, can know what it's like to be a refined man like me!?

Japanese: 裁判長！　これは‥‥、 不当な言いがかりです！
English: Your Honor! This-This is... This is unjust badgering of the witness!

Japanese: 異議は却下。
English: Objection overruled.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか 成歩堂くん！
English: Mr. Wright! What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: なぜこの証人が、詐欺グループの 電話番号を‥‥！
English: Why would the witness have the numbers of a group of con artists on his phone!?

Japanese: 決まってるじゃないですか！ モロヘイヤは‥‥
English: Isn't that obvious!? The witness is...

Japanese: そのグループのことを 調べていたんです！
English: He's investigating that group!

Japanese: は？
English: Excuse me?

Japanese: ちょちょっと、なるほどくん！ ナニ言ってるの！
English: What in the world are you saying, Nick!?

Japanese: ただ調べてただけじゃ、 殺人の動機にならないよ！
English: If he was just looking into the group, then he has no reason to kill anyone!

Japanese: なりませんよ弁護人。
English: I can not accept the defense's answer.

Japanese: （それもそうか‥‥）
English: (Yeah, I should have seen that coming...)

Japanese: もう一度、聞きます。
English: I'll ask you again:

Japanese: なぜこの証人が、こんなアヤシゲな 電話番号を‥‥？
English: Why would the witness have the numbers of a group of con artists on his phone!?

Japanese: その詐欺グループの 被害者だったのです！
English: He was victimized by this group of con artists!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだったんですか！
English: I-Is that right!?

Japanese: そして彼は復讐のために、 彼らの名前と電話番号を調べて‥‥
English: And to take his revenge, he's looking for the names and numbers of the con artists...

Japanese: ま、待ってよ！　なるほどくん。
English: Hold on, Nick! What are you talking about!?

Japanese: それじゃあ、町尾巡査さんを 殺す動機にならないよ！
English: If that's true, then he doesn't have a motive to kill Dustin Prince!

Japanese: （そ‥‥そりゃそうだな）
English: (I guess so...)

Japanese: どうしました？ 急に静かになりましたが‥‥。
English: What is it? You became quiet all of a sudden.

Japanese: すみません。 もう一度、やりなおします。
English: Um, I'm sorry, Your Honor. I'd like to try one more time.

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥。
English: *sigh* Alright.

Japanese: ではもう一度、聞きます。
English: One more time, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: なぜこの証人が、こんなアヤシゲな 電話番号を‥‥？
English: Why would the witness have the numbers of a group of con artists on his phone!?

Japanese: この詐欺グループの メンバーだったのです！
English: Mr. Wellington is a member of this very group!

Japanese: ‥‥うほぉッ！
English: Nooooo!

Japanese: あなたは、仲間の電話番号を コイツに登録していた！
English: All of your "friends'" phone numbers are stored right here on this phone.

Japanese: それを調べられてしまったら、 すべてがおしまいです！
English: If anyone were to look into these phone numbers, it would be all over for you.

Japanese: だからあなたは、殺人を犯したッ！
English: That is why you had to kill.

Japanese: も‥‥もうたくさんだあぁぁっ！！
English: Noooo! This is tooooo much!!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、 スジはとおりますね‥‥。
English: Hmm, that does make quite a bit of sense.

Japanese: どうなんですか！ モロヘイヤさん！
English: Well, Mr. Wellington? Would you care to explain?

Japanese: （よし‥‥キマったか！）
English: (I got you now!)

Japanese: ボクは‥‥あの‥‥ あの警官を‥‥
English: I... I... That... I... That police officer...

Japanese: お待ちください！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What is it, Mr. Payne?

Japanese: 裁判長！　これは‥‥これは、 その‥‥不当な言いがかりです！
English: Your Honor! This-This is... This... This is unjust badgering of the witness!

Japanese: ‥‥あなた、さっきも 言いましたね、それ。
English: You said the exact same thing only a few seconds ago.

Japanese: よよよよ、よろしいですか！
English: P-P-P-P-Please!

Japanese: 例の電話の通話内容を 思い出していただきたい！
English: Please, let's think about the content of that phone call!

Japanese: 『あ、あの、もしもし。 スズキと申しますが‥‥』
English: "Um, hello?"

Japanese: 『あ。すみません。その携帯電話を 落とした者なんですけど‥‥』
English: "Oh, thank you! I've been searching for my phone."

Japanese: 『よかった！　じゃァさっそく お会いして、お返ししますッ！』
English: "Is this yours? Oh, I'm glad you called! We can meet up and I can give this back!"

Japanese: 証人は、携帯電話を 返してもらうヤクソクをしていた！
English: The defendant had already promised that she would return the phone.

Japanese: あとは、ただ取りに行って、 彼女から電話を受けとればよい。
English: After that, all Mr. Wellington had to do was meet Ms. Byrde to get his phone back.

Japanese: なぜ、わざわざ殺人を犯す 必要があるのですかッ！
English: Why, then, would he need to kill anyone!?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 たしかに、それもそうですね。
English: Hmm... That is a valid point.

Japanese: いかがですか、弁護人。 この点については？
English: What does the defense think about this point?

Japanese: （た、たしかに‥‥）
（フツウに考えれば、 そうなんだよな‥‥）
English: (If you think about it logically, then it makes sense...)

Japanese: じゃあきっと、 フツウじゃなかったんだね！
English: Then maybe we should be thinking outside the box!

Japanese: （そのとおり。だから、 殺人事件が起こった‥‥）
English: (Yeah! If we think like that... Let's see...)

Japanese: （モロヘイヤのヤツ、 現場で何があったんだろう？）
English: (Maybe that slimeball saw something at the crime scene that made him commit murder.)

Japanese: いかがですか？　弁護人。
English: Your thoughts, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうですね。
English: Hmm, well...

Japanese: おそらく彼は、スナオに電話を 受け取りに行けなかったのです。
English: I don't think Mr. Wellington went to pick up his phone in a very friendly manner.

Japanese: でも、取りに行くヤクソクは したのですぞ！
English: But he was promised his phone, so why would he have been unfriendly to the defendant?

Japanese: ‥‥きっと現場で、何か マズいものでも見たんでしょう。
English: I think he must have seen something that didn't agree with him when he got there.

Japanese: では、うかがいましょう‥‥。
English: Well, then Mr. Wright...

Japanese: 現場で見た、マズいもの。 ‥‥なんですか？　それは。
English: What was this "something" that didn't agree with the witness?

Japanese: モロヘイヤさんは、 これを見てしまったんです！
English: The witness saw this!

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか‥‥証人？
English: Mr. Wellington. I-Is he correct?

Japanese: 思いっきり“見たことない”という カオをしているようですが‥‥？
English: By the expression on his face, I would say that wasn't it.

Japanese: （ちがったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It looks like I was wrong...)

Japanese: しかたありません。 もう一度、考えてもらいましょう。
English: Wright, Wright, Wright. Try to think before you present again.

Japanese: （‥‥モロヘイヤは、携帯電話を 受け取りに、現場まで行った）
English: (So, he went to get his cell phone back,)

Japanese: （しかし！　彼はすがたを、 あらわすことができなかった）
English: (but when he got there, he lost control of himself.)

Japanese: （彼にとって、ツゴウの悪いものが 目に入ってしまったからだ‥‥）
English: (It was probably because he saw something that was really bad for him there...)

Japanese: （その“ツゴウの悪いもの” とは‥‥！）
English: (And that "really bad thing" was...!)

Japanese: モロヘイヤさんが見たのは ‥‥もちろん、被害者です。
English: What Mr. Wellington saw was... the victim.

Japanese: ひ‥‥被害者？ 町尾　守巡査のことですか！
English: T-The... The victim!? You mean Dustin Prince!?

Japanese: 町尾巡査は、勤務を終えたカッコウ のまま、デートに来ていました。
English: Dustin Prince had gone on his date right after his shift was over.

Japanese: すなわち、警察官の制服で！
English: With no time to change, he went to the park still wearing his police uniform!

Japanese: あっ！
English: Oh!

Japanese: “電話を拾った女性が、 警察官といっしょにいる‥‥！”
English: "The girl that picked up my phone is with a policeman!"

Japanese: ‥‥まさか２人が恋人同士とは 思わない証人は、気が動転した！
English: He couldn't have known they were going out so he began to worry.

Japanese: 電話を返してもらうときに、 何か聞かれるかもしれない。
English: He was afraid the policeman would ask a few questions before returning the phone.

Japanese: “もし怪しまれてしまって、 電話を調べられたら‥‥？”
English: "If I do anything suspicious, he might run a check on my phone..."

Japanese: ‥‥いや。もしかしたら、もう 調べられている可能性だってある！
English: In his mind, it was possible they had already run a check on the phone!

Japanese: ‥‥パニックにおちいって しまったわけですか‥‥。
English: And he went into a panic, is what you're saying?

Japanese: そうです。
English: Exactly.

Japanese: 町尾　守は、警官の制服を 着ていたから殺害されたのです！
English: Officer Prince was murdered simply because he was in uniform!

Japanese: 亜内検事。 ‥‥何か、言うことは？
English: Mr. Payne. Do you have any comments?

Japanese: か‥‥考え中です‥‥。
English: I, um... I'm thinking...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 どうやら、答えが出たようですね。
English: Hmm, it seems the truth has come out at last.

Japanese: この証人、諸平野　貴雅は‥‥
English: The witness... Mr. Wellington, you are--

Japanese: うふ‥‥ふふ‥‥
English: Ha... Ah ha ha...

Japanese: わはハはワはははわはハはわははは はわはははわはハはわはわはワはハ
English: Ahahahahahahaaahahaahahaahaha hahahaHahahaAhahahahahaHahaha HahhaahaAAAHAHAhhahahahahah

Japanese: はわははははわハははわははハはわ わハわはハははワはワハははわはは
English: AHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAWAHA HAHAHAHAWAHAHAHAHAWAHA AHAHAHWAHAHAHAHAAAAAAAA

Japanese: ふふふふふ‥‥ふふ、やるねえ‥‥ 三流大学にしちゃ‥‥上デキだぁ。
English: Ha ha ha ha... Impressive... Not bad for a person with a third-rate education...

Japanese: な、何がおかしいんだ！
English: What's that supposed to mean!?

Japanese: 証拠だよ‥‥証拠ぉ！
English: The evidence... Evidence!

Japanese: ううう‥‥あのヒト、なんだか コワい‥‥っていうか、オモシロ
English: Uuugh! That guy is really creeping me out--

Japanese: キミがさっきからふり回してる、 そのアヤシイ携帯電話さあ。
English: All you've been waving around and talking about is that "suspicious" cell phone.

Japanese: 詐欺グループの電話番号が 登録されていたって？
English: Suspicious phone number this, suspicious con group that! They're all on that phone!

Japanese: それが、ボクのものだって証拠、 どこにあるってんだよぉォ！
English: But who's to say that phone is really mine!? Where's your proof!? Your evidence!?

Japanese: ‥‥この携帯電話が‥‥ きみのものである証拠‥‥？
English: You want proof that this phone is yours?

Japanese: うへへ‥‥へ‥‥うへ。 さっきも言ったけどさあ。
English: Ahahahaha! Ahehehehehe! I already told you earlier!

Japanese: ボクがなくした携帯電話はさあ、 もう見つかったんだよねぇ。
English: That phone I lost -- I've already found it!

Japanese: キミの持ってる電話が誰のものかは 知らないけどお。
English: You don't have even the slightest idea who the phone in your hand belongs to!

Japanese: ボクのじゃないことは たしかだぞコラァ！
English: You can be sure it isn't mine, you simpleton!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: へへへ‥‥へ‥‥へへへへへ‥‥ ユカイだねえ、そのあわてたカオ。
English: Hehehehe... Heh... Hehehehe... It feels good to see you squirm.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥たしかに、問題は その、アヤシイ携帯電話ですねえ。
English: Hmm... We do seem to have a problem on our hands with this phone.

Japanese: いったい誰のものか？ 証明できなければ、意味がない。
English: Whose phone is it? Without knowing that, it's meaningless as evidence.

Japanese: ま‥‥待ってください！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: （冗談じゃない！ ここで逆転されてたまるか！）
English: (This is bad... I can't let him turn the tables on me like this!!)

Japanese: （この‥‥携帯電話！）
English: (Hmm... This cell phone...)

Japanese: （何か‥‥！　何か見落としを してるはずだ！）
English: (There has to be something I've overlooked. There's got to be! Hmm... Maybe...)

Japanese: こいつには、たしかに詐欺グループ の電話番号が登録されている！
English: This phone has the names and numbers of those in the con group in its memory.

Japanese: 裁判長！ 今、お見せしましょう！
English: I can show them to you, Your Honor!

Japanese: ヤレヤレ。 ナニ言ってるんだいキミ！
English: I don't believe this. What are you talking about!?
Uh...

Japanese: 今、問題になっているのは、 《電話の持ち主は誰か？》だよ！
English: What we are trying to determine is who that phone belongs to!

Japanese: 登録された番号なんて、 なんの意味もない！
English: Who cares about what phone numbers are stored on it!?

Japanese: そんなの、キミが勝手に 登録したモノかもしれないしね！
English: Besides, who knows. Maybe you went and added some of those numbers in yourself!

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりですね。 今の発言は、認めません！
English: The witness is quite right. I'm afraid I have to reject the defense's proposal.

Japanese: （くそ。モロヘイヤのヤツ、 復活しちまったぞ‥‥）
English: (Grr... That jerk is back to his arrogant, annoying self again...)

Japanese: そうだ！　この電話に残っている 指紋を調べればいいんだ！
English: I got it! We should check for fingerprints!

Japanese: ‥‥指紋、ですか‥‥。
English: Finger...prints...?

Japanese: きっと、モロヘイヤの指紋が 残っているはずです！
English: Yes, Your Honor. Mr. Wellington must have left some prints on this phone!

Japanese: な、ナニ言ってるの、 なるほどくん！
English: Nick! Don't you remember!?

Japanese: マコちゃんからもらうとき、 なるほどくん、ふいたじゃない。
English: When you got that from Maggey, you wiped it off!

Japanese: え。
English: I what!?

Japanese: 砂でヨゴれているからって。
English: You said there was sand all over it, so...

Japanese: ふ‥‥ふいちゃったの？ ‥‥ぼくが？
English: W-Wiped it? I wiped it...?

Japanese: それはもう、念入りに。
English: Pretty thoroughly, too...

Japanese: ひゃあーっはっはっはっはぁ！
English: WahahaHahaHAHahaha!

Japanese: ユカイだねえ。三流のゴミたちが ドタバタするサマをながめるのは！
English: It's oh-so-much fun watching third-rate trash babble like morons amongst themselves!

Japanese: （くそッ！　カンゼンに 立ち直っちまったぞ‥‥）
English: (Aaargh! He's made a complete recovery...)

Japanese: 何度も言うけど、ボクの携帯電話は ここにあるからねえ！
English: How many times do I have to say this: my phone is right here! You see?

Japanese: ちなみに、この電話に登録されてる 番号は、見ることができないよ。
English: Oh, and incidentally, you can't check the numbers stored on this phone.

Japanese: 急に故障したみたいでね。 ‥‥ゼンブ消えちゃったんだよ！
English: It must have glitched because all the numbers just magically disappeared!

Japanese: （‥‥！　証拠になる番号を 消去しやがったな‥‥！）
English: (You've got to be joking! He erased all the numbers I was going to use as evidence!)

Japanese: ‥‥モロヘイヤさん。
English: Mr. Wellington...

Japanese: オヤオヤ‥‥。
English: What's this?

Japanese: ボクに向かって口をきく勇気が まだ、残っていたとはねえ。
English: From the way you talk to me, it sounds like you still have some fight left in you.

Japanese: ケッキョク、あなたの携帯電話は どこで見つかったんですか？
English: Where did you finally find your cell phone!?

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥
English: ...Heh heh heh...

Japanese: あーっはっはっはっはっはっはっ！ ‥‥コイツはケッサクだ！
English: Ah ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha! Oh, you are too much!

Japanese: モチロン、キミはそれを 知らないワケだねえ！
English: And of course you have no idea what I'm talking about!

Japanese: お‥‥思い出した‥‥ ‥‥思い出したぞ‥‥！ I... I...
English: Oh my g-- NOW I REMEMBER!!

Japanese: あれ。 ‥‥切れちまった‥‥か。
English: Huh, looks like they hung up.

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ‥‥ やっと見つけたよ‥‥。
English: Ah, good. I finally found it.

Japanese: （‥‥あのときだったのか‥‥）
English: (So that's when...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうかしたかな？　センセイ。 そんな目でニラんじゃって‥‥。
English: What's wrong, Mr. Attorney? Why the harsh glare in your eyes...?

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ‥‥このままじゃあ‥‥
English: Nick! We've worked so hard to get this far, but

Japanese: 逃げられちゃうよ！ ここまできたのに！
English: if you don't do something quick, he's going to get off scot-free!

Japanese: わかってる。
English: I know.

Japanese: （‥‥でも、この携帯電話が あいつのものだなんて‥‥）
English: (I know this phone has to be his...)

Japanese: （どうやって 証明すればいい‥‥！）
English: (But how am I supposed to prove something like that!?)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: その携帯電話の持ち主が 特定できない以上、
English: If you cannot prove who the owner of that cell phone is,

Japanese: あなたの告発には なんのチカラもありません。
English: your indictment has no basis, and therefore, no power.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、あと１歩、 およばなかったようですね‥‥。
English: It looks like you came up a penny short.

Japanese: （どこかで‥‥ どこかでまちがったのか‥‥？）
English: (Where...? Where did I go wrong...?)

Japanese: ま。三流弁護士にしては よくやったほうだと思うよ。
English: Don't blame yourself, you're merely a third-rate lawyer.

Japanese: キミのシッパイは、ただ１つ。
English: You only made one big mistake.

Japanese: 自分がナニモノなのか？ それをわきまえなかったコトさ。
English: Who are you? What are you? That's something you haven't figured out for yourself yet.

Japanese: （自分が‥‥ ナニモノなのか‥‥？）
English: (Who... I am?)

Japanese: それでは、この証人に対する 尋問を終了します！
English: The court hereby concludes the cross-examination!

Japanese: フッフッフッ‥‥。
English: Heh heh heh...

Japanese: じゃ、ボクはそろそろ シツレイさせてもらいますよ。
English: If that will be all, I'll have to bid you gentlemen and ladies goodbye.

Japanese: 一流のレストランに 予約を入れてるものでね‥‥。
English: I have a reservation at that ultra-fancy restaurant on the upper side of town.

Japanese: おつかれさまでした。 ‥‥どうぞ、よいお食事を。
English: Thank you for your assistance. You've had a stressful day, so please, bon appetite!

Japanese: （‥‥どうする！ このままでいいのか‥‥？）
English: (What am I supposed to do!? Am I supposed to just let it go at that?)

Japanese: （ダメだ！　ぼくには もう、何もできない‥‥）
English: (It's no use. I can't do any more. There's nothing left.)

Japanese: （イナカへ帰るしか‥‥。 でも、ぼくのイナカ、どこだった
English: (Nothing left but to go back to my hometown... But where in the world is that, anyway?)

Japanese: ナニ言ってるの！
English: What are you mumbling about!?

Japanese: なるほどくんは‥‥ なるほどくんなら‥‥
English: You're... Well, you're YOU, Nick! And because you're you,

Japanese: こんなところで あきらめちゃダメだよッ！
English: you can't give up now!

Japanese: （“なるほどくんなら”‥‥？）
English: ("If I were me"...?)

Japanese: じゃあこんなとき、ぼくなら どうするんだろう？
English: Alright. If I were me, then what would I do?

Japanese: 決まってるでしょ！ 叫ぶんだよ‥‥とりあえず！
English: That's easy! For starters, you'd raise your voice and object!

Japanese: （とりあえず、叫ぶか！）
English: (So for now, I should at least say something!)

Japanese: お待ちください、裁判長！
English: Please wait, Your Honor!

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん！
English: Alright, Nick!

Japanese: もしかしたら‥‥、 証明できるかもしれません！
English: I think I may be able to prove it!

Japanese: “証明”‥‥？ 何を、ですかな？
English: "Prove it...?" Prove what, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥すべてを！
English: Everything!

Japanese: さ、裁判長！ すでに尋問は終了しております！
English: Y-Your Honor! The cross- examination has already ended!

Japanese: これ以上、わずらわしい 質問で、証人を‥‥
English: If he questions the witness with any more of his badgering...

Japanese: 苦しめるわけには いきませんね、弁護人。
English: You will not harass the witness. Is that clear, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: いかないんだよ、センセイ。
English: Did you hear that? No harassment allowed, Mr. Attorney.

Japanese: おねがいします！　裁判長！
English: Please, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 では、１つだけ。
English: Very well. But this is your last chance, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 証拠品を提示することを 許可しましょう。
English: You may present one piece of evidence to the court.
(I only get one shot at this!)

Japanese: それで、すべてを“証明” できなければ‥‥
English: If you cannot "prove" everything...

Japanese: おしまいです。 依頼人も、あなた自身も。
English: It's over. For your client, and for you.

Japanese: それでよろしいかな？
English: Do you fully understand?

Japanese: ‥‥‥わかりまし
English: Yes, Your Hon--

Japanese: 裁判長！　おわかりですか！ 尋問はすでに終了しているんだ！
English: I'm sure you are well aware, Your Honor, but the cross- examination period has ended!

Japanese: あなたこそ、おわかりかな？
English: Were you paying attention, Mr. Payne?

Japanese: ‥‥私は、証拠品の提示を 許可すると言っているのです。
English: I said that Mr. Wright could present only one more piece of evidence.

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Oh...

Japanese: では、成歩堂くん。 ‥‥最後のチャンスです。
English: Now then, Mr. Wright. This is your last chance.

Japanese: （これで‥‥キメなければ！ ナニがナンでも‥‥！）
English: (It all comes down to this! It's Go time!)

Japanese: あなたの考えをすべて証明する 証拠品を１つ。‥‥お見せなさい！
English: Please present the one piece of evidence that will explain everything!

Japanese: それがあなたの 最終的な“答え”ですか‥‥。
English: Is this your final answer?

Japanese: ガッカリですね。
English: It's a bit disappointing.

Japanese: い、いやいや！ 今のは、軽いごアイサツです！
English: Nonono! That was just a friendly gesture!

Japanese: ヤレヤレ。‥‥ずいぶんな ごアイサツもあったもんだ。
English: Ha ha ha. You sure know how to send a friendly gesture to me, at least.

Japanese: これは、あなたにとって 最後のチャンスなのですぞ！
English: This is your absolute last chance, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: アイサツ抜きでおねがいします！
English: So no more of these "friendly gestures"!

Japanese: スミマセン‥‥。
English: Yes, Your Honor... Sorry...

Japanese: ‥‥これはどうも、 ごていねいに。
English: Why, thank you. How nice.

Japanese: ワタクシは、こういうモノです。
English: Here, please have one of mine.

Japanese: 証拠品《裁判長の名刺》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Judge's Business Card added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥って、名刺交換 してる場合じゃありません！
English: Wait, what am I doing!? This isn't the time to be exchanging business cards!

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: その名刺のどこが重要なのか？ それは‥‥
English: There is something very important about that card. And that is...

Japanese: その名刺の名前は、ぼくが ダレなのかを教えてくれます！
English: The name on that card tells people who I am!

Japanese: このぼくこそが “成歩堂　龍一”である、と！
English: It even told ME that I'm "Phoenix Wright"!

Japanese: ‥‥知らなかったんですか？
English: ...Did you not know that?

Japanese: はい！
English: Nope!

Japanese: ‥‥な、な、な‥‥ なんなんですかコレは！
English: Wh-Wh-What's the meaning of this nonsense!?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん！ イイカゲンにしなさいッ！
English: Mr. Wright! Get a hold of yourself and start behaving like a proper lawyer!

Japanese: お、大目玉だよ なるほどくん！
English: Ouch... Talk about a tongue-lashing, Nick...

Japanese: （‥‥方向を変えたほうが よさそうだな‥‥）
English: (Urk. I should probably try this from a different angle...)

Japanese: ダイジなのは、その名刺の ウラ面なのです！
English: This card is important because of what is on the back!

Japanese: ウラ面って、なるほどくんの 携帯電話の番号が書いてあるけど。
English: Hmm? You wrote your cell phone number on the back, but...

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: But that's exactly it.

Japanese: この番号、かけてみてくれないか？ ‥‥きみの電話から。
English: Can you please call this number from your cell phone?

Japanese: え？　‥‥今？ でも、審理中だよ。
English: Huh? Right now!? But court is still in session!

Japanese: かまわないから。
English: It's OK. You'll see.

Japanese: いいのかなあ。
English: OK, if you say so.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。 どういうことですか？
English: Is the defense preparing something, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: これから、ぼくの携帯電話に かけてみます。
English: We are going to call my cell phone now.

Japanese: ‥‥それで、 この事件はすべて解決です。
English: And then the court will see everything for what it is!

Japanese: そ、そんなバカなコトが‥‥
English: O-Of all the idiotic, stupid things to...
Aaagh!

Japanese: なな、なんだ。 なんでボクの電話が‥‥？
English: Wh-What!? Why is my phone...?

Japanese: ‥‥しかもなんだ！　この、 バカみたいな着信メロディーは！
English: And what is with this stupid- sounding ringtone!?

Japanese: モロヘイヤさん‥‥
English: Mr. Wellington.

Japanese: おかしいですねえ。
English: Hmm... How strange...

Japanese: それ‥‥。 ぼくの携帯電話じゃないですか！
English: I could almost swear that you're holding MY phone!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと‥‥
English: Y-Your...

Japanese: あああああああッ！ ま、ま、ま、まさか‥‥！
English: AAAAAAAAAAAH! No, no, no, no, no! It can't...!!

Japanese: ‥‥今朝は、後頭部に一発、 どうもありがとう。
English: By the way, before I forget, thank you very much for the lump on my head this morning.

Japanese: う‥‥うううう‥‥
English: Nnnnngh...

Japanese: ‥‥もう、説明するまでも ないだろう？
English: I don't think I need to explain any further, except to say:

Japanese: きみはあのとき、持っていく 携帯電話をまちがえたんだよ！
English: When you went to retrieve your cell phone, you mistakenly took the wrong one!
...Mmgh...

Japanese: わああああああああああああああ あああああああああああああああ
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa

Japanese: あああああああああああああああ あああああああああああああああ
English: aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa

Japanese: あああああアアアアアアアアアア アアアアアアアアアアアアアアア
