English: aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaAAAAAA AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA

Japanese: アアアアアアアアアアアァァァァ ァァァァァァァァァァァァァァッ！
English: AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA AAAAAaaaaaaaagggggggghhhnn!

Japanese: ‥‥そうだったんですか。 モロヘイヤに殴られて‥‥。
English: So that is what happened. You were knocked out by Mr. Wellington...

Japanese: 彼は、プライドとメンツだけで 生きていた男でした。
English: He is a man who lives on his pride and self-image alone.

Japanese: 詐欺グループのメンバーで あることをかくすために、
English: And in order to hide his involvement with the con artists' group,

Japanese: 正常な判断力を なくしてしまったんでしょう。
English: he has become paranoid, and has lost all ability to make rational judgments.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ、成歩堂くん。 キミが持ってる、その電話は‥‥
English: Then... Then, Mr. Wright... The phone you're holding...

Japanese: モチロン、これがモロヘイヤの ものだったんですよ。
English: It's Mr. Wellington's, naturally.

Japanese: 亜内検事。 モロヘイヤ氏は‥‥？
English: Speaking of that man, how is he, Mr. Payne?

Japanese: はい‥‥。 緊急逮捕いたしました。
English: Ah, he was arrested and has been taken away, Your Honor.

Japanese: よろしい。
English: Very well.

Japanese: では、被告人・須々木　マコに 判決を言いわたしましょう。
English: Now then, this court finds the defendant, Maggey Byrde...

Japanese: ‥‥では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!

Japanese: 同日　午後　２時１６分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: September 8, 2:16 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: やっぱり、成歩堂さんは 成歩堂さんだったッス！
English: I knew that the real you would shine through eventually!

Japanese: スズキ、モーレツにカンドーッス！
English: I am so moved by what you've done for me, sir!

Japanese: 本当に どーもありがとうございましたッ！
English: Thank you so much, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 町尾巡査、カワイソウだったね。 なんにも悪くないのに‥‥。
English: I feel really bad for Dustin. He didn't do anything to deserve this...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥それ、 アタシのせいかもしれません‥‥。
English: It's probably because of me...

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: スズキの人生は、まさに不運と 敗北と大番狂わせの見本市でした。
English: My whole life has been nothing but a whirlwind of bad luck and failures.

Japanese: み、見本市。
English: Your whole life? It couldn't have been that bad, could it?

Japanese: 生後６ヶ月のとき、マンションの ９階から落っこちたのを皮切りに、
English: Since I was 6 months old, when I fell from the 9th floor of my apartment building,

Japanese: ひととおりの乗り物にはひかれ、 ひととおりの食べ物にはあたり、
English: I've been hit by all sorts of vehicles, gotten sick from all sorts of foods,

Japanese: だいたいの試験には落第して、 ほとんどの災害をも経験して、
English: failed at almost every test I've ever taken, experienced almost every kind of disaster,

Japanese: ジャンケンも勝ったことはおろか、 アイコになったことすらないッス。
English: and never won or even tied at a game of tic-tac-toe!

Japanese: ‥‥まさに、 負けつづけの人生ッス。
English: My life has really been nothing but a string of disasters.

Japanese: て‥‥テッテーしてるね それは‥‥。
English: That is, uh... pretty bad...

Japanese: 小・中・高校と、一貫した あだ名が《不幸の女神》。
English: Up until I went to college, I was known as the "Goddess of Misfortune".

Japanese: 警察学校では《堕天使》と 呼ばれていたッス。
English: And then, at the academy, everyone called me "Lady Luckless".

Japanese: “だてんし”‥‥
English: "Lady Luckless"...

Japanese: しかも、自分の不幸は 他人まで巻きこむみたいで‥‥
English: What's worse is that my misfortune always seems to latch onto those around me.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうコト？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: アタシ、ヒトが困ってるのを 見ると、ついおセッカイを‥‥
English: When I see someone in trouble, I always try to help...

Japanese: ああ、さっきもそんなコト 言ってたね。
English: Ah, that's right. You were talking about this earlier.

Japanese: こないだもそうッス。
English: It happened again recently, too, sir.

Japanese: 横断歩道でおばあちゃんが オロオロしてたッス。
English: There was an old lady pacing back and forth by the pedestrian crosswalk.

Japanese: 手を引いてあげて‥‥
English: I gave her my hand and...

Japanese: 気がついたら、アタシの家で 晩ゴハンごちそうしてたッス。
English: before I knew it, we were having dinner at my house.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Oh.

Japanese: きっと町尾さんも アタシと関わったせいで‥‥
English: I'm sure that Dustin's gone because of me...

Japanese: そ、そんなコトないよ！
English: That's not true!

Japanese: あのグローブも たいした意味はなかったッス。
English: That glove didn't even have any sort of special meaning.

Japanese: １回、夜勤をかわってもらった お礼だったのに‥‥。
English: It was just a present to say thanks for covering one of my night shifts.

Japanese: そうだったんだ‥‥。
English: Oh, I see...

Japanese: みんな、アタシのせいッス！ 町尾さんのことも、
English: Everything is all my fault! Dustin's death,

Japanese: 成歩堂さんのアタマが ダメになったのも！
English: your head being all messed up...

Japanese: あの。ぼくのアタマはまだ、 ダメになったワケじゃあ‥‥
English: Uh, well, I don't think my head is that messed up yet...

Japanese: ‥‥スズキ、これから 第２の人生を探してみるッス。
English: I'm going to find a new life for myself starting now.

Japanese: 次に会うときまでには、 きっと‥‥
English: The next time we meet, I'm sure I'll...

Japanese: スズキ、デッカイ運を つかんでみせるッス！
English: I'm sure I'll have found a whole ocean's worth of good luck by then, sir!

Japanese: ああ。《不幸の女神》なんて 冗談じゃないもんな！
English: Yeah. After all, the "Goddess of Misfortune" is only a name!

Japanese: そッス！ ヤクソクッス！
English: You bet! I'm gonna make it! I promise!

Japanese: 次に会うときは《不幸なヒト》 レベルに登りつめておくッス！
English: Next time we meet, I'll only be an "Unlucky Person", instead of a goddess!

Japanese: そ、そうとも！
English: Y-Yeah! That's the spirit!

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、成歩堂さん。 真宵さん。アタシはこれで。
English: Well, Mr. Wright, Maya, I should get going.

Japanese: うん！　‥‥がんばろうね！
English: OK! Good luck to you!

Japanese: お２人とも、お元気でッ！
English: Thanks! You take care of yourselves, too!

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。 ヒドい１日だったな‥‥。
English: *sigh* What a horrible day...

Japanese: 記憶がもどったと言っても、 まだ、少しボンヤリしてるし。
English: I've gotten my memory back, but things are still a little fuzzy...

Japanese: でも、ブジでよかったよね。 ホント、心配しちゃった！
English: But you're OK, and that's what counts. You really had me worried!

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、 あたしたちも事務所に帰ろ。
English: Come on, let's go back to the office.

Japanese: （うーん‥‥これを尋ねると、 エラいコトになりそうだけど）
English: (Hmm... I'm afraid to ask, but here goes...)

Japanese: あの‥‥悪いんだけどさ‥‥。 どなたでしたっけ？　きみ。
English: So, this might sound bad, but... uh... Who are you...?

Japanese: なによ！
English: What!?

Japanese: キオク、もどってないじゃないっ！
English: I thought you said you got your memory back!

Japanese: （その瞬間‥‥）
English: (At that moment,)

Japanese: （ぼくはほんとうに、 すべてを思い出した‥‥）
English: (everything really did come back to me...)

Japanese: イトノコ刑事‥‥。
English: Detective Gumshoe...

Japanese: いろんな事件で戦った、 敵ながらあっぱれな男。
English: He's someone I've had clashes with in the past during certain cases.

Japanese: 味方になってくれた 事件もあったっけ‥‥
English: But he's also been a good ally during others.

Japanese: 裁判長‥‥。
English: The Judge...

Japanese: すぐに人の意見に左右される、 愛すべきじいさん。
English: He's a lovable, kind old man who is easily swayed by other people's opinions.

Japanese: 最終的には、いつも正しい 判断をしてくれるんだよな‥‥
English: But in the end, he always comes up with the right verdict.

Japanese: ‥‥この人は‥‥ 知らないな。
English: This person... I haven't got a clue...

Japanese: ぼくを知ってるみたい だったけど、
English: He seems to know me, but

Japanese: きっと人ちがいだろう‥‥
English: maybe he's mistaking me for someone else...?

Japanese: （そして、この子‥‥） ‥‥真宵ちゃん‥‥
English: (And this girl...) ...Maya...?

Japanese: あっ！ ‥‥やっと思い出してくれたよ！
English: You... You finally remembered!

Japanese: （綾里　真宵（あやさとまよい） ‥‥ぼくの助手だ）
English: (This is Maya Fey, my assistant.)

Japanese: （彼女については‥‥そう。 忘れられない思い出だらけだ）
English: (That's right... I have so many unforgettable memories about her.)

Japanese: （たとえば‥‥）
English: (For example...)

Japanese: なーるーほーどーくん！ どうしたの？
English: Earth to Nick! What's wrong?

Japanese: あたしのカオばっかり見て！ やっぱり、なつかしい？
English: You keep staring at me! Don't tell me you've missed me?

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。
English: Uh, well, yeah I suppose I have.

Japanese: ずいぶん、ひさしぶりに 会ったような気がするよ。
English: I feel like I haven't seen you in ages.

Japanese: そうなんだ。
English: Oh?

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、 これからもよろしくね！
English: Well, I'm back now. So it's time for us to create new memories together!

Japanese: うん‥‥。 こちらこそ、よろしく。
English: Alright. Sounds good.

Japanese: （携帯電話のメモリーも モロヘイヤに消されちまったし）
English: (All the phone numbers on my phone were erased by Mr. Wellington.)

Japanese: （ぼくもまた、 最初から出直しだな‥‥）
English: (I guess I have to start over from the very beginning...)

Japanese: ほらほら、なるほどくん。 いつものラーメン屋、行くよ！
English: Come on, Nick! Let's go to our usual burger joint!

Japanese: ハイハイ。
English: OK, OK.

Japanese: （ジッサイ、彼女に再会してから まだ２ヶ月しかたっていない）
English: (Actually, it hasn't even been two months since she came back into my life.)

Japanese: （あれは、そう‥‥）
English: (And that story...)

Japanese: （２ヶ月前の、 ある雨の午後の話だ‥‥）
English: (That story began on one rainy afternoon, two months ago...)

Japanese: ■第一話　失われた逆転 おわり
English: Episode 1: The Lost Turnabout THE END

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: This is a clear-cut case with little reason for doubt,

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: and further deliberation will not be required here.

Japanese: 被告人、スズキ　マコに たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Maggey Byrde...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
